title: What Does Your Soulmate Look Like?
description: Through simple questions, dive deep into your subconscious to find the soul fragment that best matches yours.

questions.text: If you walked into a mysterious forest, what would you notice first?

options.text: Gentle sunlight filtering through the leaves
Soft, moss-covered ground beneath your feet
The call of an unknown bird in the distance
The scent of flowers and grass drifting in the air

questions.text: Deep in the forest, there's a wooden cabin. The person who opens the door gives you what kind of feeling?

options.text: Warm with a welcoming smile
Quiet with deep, thoughtful eyes
Cheerful and full of energy
Mysterious and wise

questions.text: The person hands you a drink. What is it?

options.text: A steaming cup of sweet hot cocoa
A light, subtly sweet hot tea
A colorful fruit juice
Sparkling water in an elegant glass

questions.text: A painting hangs on the cabin wall. What scene does it depict?

options.text: Two silhouettes cuddling by a fireplace
A calm, still lake
A group of people laughing at a meadow picnic
A vast, deep starry sky

questions.text: You find a treasure box in the cabin. What's inside?

options.text: A warm, soft scarf
A yellowed handwritten diary
A map leading to an unknown land
A crystal ball emitting a faint glow

questions.text: As you leave, the person gives you a gift. What do you think it is?

options.text: A dried flower
A handmade bookmark
A small compass
A miniature mirror

questions.text: On your way back, you encounter a fork in the road. How do you choose?

options.text: Take the safest and most reliable path
Take the quietest, least traveled path
Take the most interesting-looking path
Close your eyes and follow your intuition

questions.text: It starts raining on the way back. What do you do?

options.text: Take shelter under someone's eaves
Open the umbrella you brought along
Follow your instinct and run home quickly
Enjoy the feeling of raindrops on your skin

questions.text: After the rain stops, what appears in the sky?

options.text: A rainbow stretching across the horizon
A brilliant sunset breaking through the clouds
A few butterflies dancing gracefully
The first star shining at the edge of the sky

questions.text: After returning home, what's the first thing you want to do?

options.text: Call your closest person to share today's adventure
Quietly take a hot bath and organize your thoughts
Look through photos and plan the next adventure
Write today's feelings in a diary

results.title: The Morning Light Guardian
results.description: You inwardly crave a stable and gentle relationship. Your ideal partner is someone who provides firm support when you're vulnerable, someone as warm as morning light. They don't need fancy words — just a tender gaze and arms that are always open for you.
results.comment: Tips: Your current pace is great, but occasionally showing your vulnerable side can deepen your relationship.

results.title: The Deep Sea Listener
results.description: You need a partner who can have soul-deep conversations with you. They may not talk much, but they can read your every glance and give you the deepest understanding and acceptance. Between you, silence can also be a comfortable form of communication.
results.comment: Tips: Your tolerance is the greatest lubricant in a relationship, but remember that putting your own feelings first is equally important.

results.title: The Breeze Companion
results.description: You yearn for freedom and novelty. Your soulmate will be someone full of vitality who can explore the unknown with you, grow together, and share life's joys. You'll create countless memories together, becoming each other's best travel companion.
results.comment: Tips: Your passionate love can be contagious — try to slow down after the excitement to listen to your partner's subtle voice.

results.title: The Starlight Inspirer
results.description: You're drawn to wisdom and depth. Your ideal partner is someone with unique perspectives who constantly inspires you to see the world through a wider lens. Your conversations are always full of sparks, and your souls collide to create the most beautiful light.
results.comment: Tips: While pursuing the ultimate love, try to give each other some breathing room — distance can sometimes create incredible beauty.

title: What Animal Are You in Love?
description: Through scenario-based Q&A, discover the animal archetype hidden within your romantic personality.

questions.text: On a weekend afternoon, what would you most like to do?

options.text: Cuddle on the couch watching movies with your partner
Go to a café alone to read
Join a friend gathering for some fun
Go hiking or camping outdoors

questions.text: Your partner suddenly gives you a small gift. Your first reaction?

options.text: Happily hug them
Calmly say thanks while secretly rejoicing inside
Excitedly take a photo and post it on social media
Start thinking about what to buy in return

questions.text: When you have an argument with your partner, you usually…

options.text: Apologize first to keep the peace
Give the silent treatment, wait for them to come to you
Voice your dissatisfaction loudly
Analyze rationally, focus on who's right and who's wrong

questions.text: They accidentally break something you cherish. You would…

options.text: Feel sad but still say it's okay
Sulk silently
Directly complain about their carelessness
Ask them to buy a new one to compensate

questions.text: What do you think is most important in a relationship?

options.text: Mutual companionship and reliance
Maintaining individual independence
Creating happy memories together
Shared goals and values

questions.text: Your partner works late and comes home very late. You would…

options.text: Wait up and prepare a late-night snack for them
Go to bed first, everyone has their own rhythm
Text them to come home soon
Start worrying if something happened

questions.text: What kind of date do you prefer?

options.text: Walking hand in hand in the park, chatting
Each reading a book, occasionally exchanging glances
Going to an amusement park or escape room
Planning a future trip together

questions.text: How do you feel about 'acting cute' in a relationship?

options.text: Super natural! Want to be cute every day
Occasionally fine, but usually feels awkward
Usually express affection in a funny way instead
Prefer showing love through actions rather than words

questions.text: Your partner's friends invite them out without including you. How do you feel?

options.text: A bit disappointed, wish to be invited along
Great, finally some time to myself
Don't mind, make plans with your own friends too
Understand, but secretly curious about what they're doing

questions.text: If they say 'I need some space', your reaction is?

options.text: Worry if you did something wrong
Completely understand, think it's perfectly normal
Say it's fine, but feel uneasy inside
Respect their wish and use the time for self-improvement

results.title: The Clingy Puppy
results.description: In love, you crave tons of security and love being close to your partner. Even the smallest gesture can fill you with happiness. You're a tender and caring lover who always puts your partner first, but remember to keep some space for yourself too.
results.comment: Tips: Your current pace is great, but occasionally showing vulnerability can make your bond even deeper.

results.title: The Proud Tsundere Cat
results.description: You seem indifferent on the surface, but deep down you crave attention. You don't like expressing love directly but show tenderness in subtle ways. You need someone who can read the meaning behind your silence to truly enter your heart.
results.comment: Tips: Your tolerance is a great relationship lubricant, but remember that prioritizing your own feelings is equally important.

results.title: The Energetic Golden Retriever
results.description: You're full of passion, always bringing joy to your partner. When it comes to love, you're straightforward and never hide your feelings. You're a sunny, cheerful lover whose energy is infectious — every day with you is filled with laughter.
results.comment: Tips: Your passionate love is contagious — try to slow down and listen to your partner's subtle voice after the excitement.

results.title: The Independent Lone Wolf
results.description: You value personal space and autonomy highly, preferring not to rely too much on others. But once you've found your person, you show extreme devotion and loyalty. You prove your love through actions rather than words — a steady and profound lover.
results.comment: Tips: While pursuing the ideal romance, try to leave some breathing room for each other — distance can sometimes create incredible beauty.

title: What's Your Love Language in Flowers?
description: Each flower represents a different feeling. Discover the unique flower language that defines your love.

questions.text: Imagine walking into a flower shop. What scent greets you first?

options.text: Rich, alluring rose fragrance
Fresh, delicate jasmine scent
Sunny sunflower aroma
Elegant, cool lily fragrance

questions.text: You're choosing a bouquet for your partner. What main color?

options.text: Passionate, romantic reds
Pure, flawless whites
Bright, warm yellows
Mysterious, noble purples

questions.text: What wrapping material would you choose for the bouquet?

options.text: Glamorous, shiny ribbon
Simple, textured kraft paper
Colorful, vibrant tulle
Elegant, frosted paper

questions.text: What kind of card would you attach when sending flowers?

options.text: A card full of sweet words
Just a simple blessing
A card with cute drawings
A card with poetry or famous quotes

questions.text: How do you think your partner would react to receiving flowers?

options.text: Moved to tears, hugging you tightly
A gentle smile, saying thank you
Screaming with surprise, immediately taking photos
Carefully examining, praising how beautiful they are

questions.text: If flowers could talk, what would you want them to say for you?

options.text: "I will love you forever"
"Thank you for always being there"
"Every day with you is so happy"
"You're the most special person I've ever met"

questions.text: Where would you place the flowers at home?

options.text: Bedroom nightstand, so you see them when you wake up
On the desk, quietly accompanying your work
The most visible spot in the living room
By the window, letting sunlight fall on the petals

questions.text: After the flowers wither, what would you do?

options.text: Make them into dried flowers to keep forever
Take a photo as a memory, then let them go
Buy a new beautiful bouquet
Press a petal into your journal as a memento

questions.text: If you could design a new flower species, what trait would it have?

options.text: Blooms only under moonlight — a romantic nocturnal flower
Changes color with the season — beautiful in every form
Can sing — spreading joy to everyone around
Glows softly at night — a gentle beacon of light

questions.text: What does a 'perfect bouquet' mean to you?

options.text: Full of romantic roses, representing undying love
A clean, simple single stem, representing quiet companionship
Colorful mixed bouquet, like a party of happiness
Rare, hard-to-find species, representing one-of-a-kind love

results.title: Rose — Undying Passion
results.description: Your love is like a burning flame, full of passion and romance. You're willing to give everything for love and believe that sincere and steady devotion is the greatest expression of romance. Your love is direct and powerful, like a red rose in full bloom.
results.comment: Tips: Your passionate and dedicated love is admirable, but remember not to lose yourself. The best love allows both people to grow.

results.title: Jasmine — Silent Companionship
results.description: Your love is like a jasmine fragrance — not overwhelming, but ever-present. You believe the best love permeates daily life, and you show your care through subtle actions. You don't need grand gestures; your consistent presence is the full expression of your love.
results.comment: Tips: Your quiet love is very touching, but don't be afraid to express your feelings directly once in a while — your partner would love to hear your inner words.

results.title: Sunflower — Joyful Radiance
results.description: Your love is like sunshine itself, bringing warmth and joy to your partner. With an optimistic and cheerful personality, you make your lover feel happy and uplifted. Your love is direct, warm, and energetic — like a sunflower always facing the sun.
results.comment: Tips: Your joyful love energy is incredible, but remember to also create some quiet, intimate moments for just the two of you.

results.title: Lily — Transcendent Elegance
results.description: Your love is like a white lily — noble, elegant, and unforgettable. You seek a love that transcends the material, pursuing deep soul connection and spiritual resonance. Your love has weight and depth — understated yet captivating, like a lingering fragrance.
results.comment: Tips: Your love pursuit is admirable. While seeking soulmates, don't forget that everyday life's little moments are a part of love too — sometimes imperfection is true perfection.

title: Love Attachment Style Test
description: Do you tend toward anxiety or avoidance in relationships? Discover your attachment tendency through this quiz.

questions.text: Your new partner hasn't replied to your message for 3 hours. Your first thought?

options.text: They must be busy, I'll wait patiently
Maybe they haven't noticed — I'll do something else first
Are they ignoring me? Should I send another?
My mind starts racing with all kinds of scenarios

questions.text: Your partner wants to go on a solo trip. Your reaction?

options.text: Fully support them; personal growth time is important
A bit reluctant but won't stop them
Ask carefully if they want to include you
Feel uneasy; worry they might meet someone else

questions.text: In a new relationship, do you initiate sharing your inner world?

options.text: Naturally share when it feels right
Wait for a deeper connection before opening up
Often want to share but worry about burdening them
Find it difficult; building walls comes more naturally

questions.text: When saying goodbye after a date, you usually feel…

options.text: Happy about today, looking forward to next time
Relieved to finally have your own space again
Reluctant, wishing you could spend more time together
Anxious, already worried about when you'll meet again

questions.text: Your partner says 'You're really important to me.' Your inner reaction?

options.text: Feel touched and sincerely happy
Feel a bit pressured, unsure if you can match that
Then immediately think: Am I important enough?
Overwhelming happiness mixed with fear of losing it

questions.text: In your ideal relationship, how much personal time would you want?

options.text: Good balance of together time and alone time
More alone time; I need lots of personal space
Prefer spending most time together
Hard to decide; afraid being apart means growing distant

questions.text: How do you usually handle conflicts in relationships?

options.text: Calmly discuss, treating it as a chance for growth
Need to cool down first, then discuss rationally
Fear conflict will ruin the relationship, try to avoid it
Emotions get intense and hard to control

questions.text: When your partner praises another person, you feel…

options.text: Normal, everyone has their strengths
A slight twinge, but you won't show it
Instantly compare yourself — am I not good enough?
Jealousy that's hard to contain

questions.text: How much time after meeting someone would you ideally confirm a relationship?

options.text: Take time to get to know each other; no rush
Hope to keep things ambiguous a while; it's fun
If you feel right, you'd want to define it quickly
Fear rejection, so unsure when to bring it up

questions.text: If you feel insecure, what would you most likely do?

options.text: Honestly communicate your feelings with your partner
Hide it. You'll handle your own emotions
Repeatedly seek reassurance from your partner
Test them through indirect actions to see if they care

results.title: Secure Attachment
results.description: You have the healthiest attachment style! In relationships, you can be honest with your feelings, trust yourself and your partner. You don't rely excessively on others for security, nor do you fear intimacy. You're comfortable with both closeness and independence.
results.comment: Tips: Your emotional stability is your greatest asset. Keep being open and trusting — this will attract equally healthy relationship partners.

results.title: Avoidant Attachment
results.description: You tend to protect yourself through emotional distance. In intimate relationships, you value your personal space, sometimes appear emotionally detached, but it doesn't mean you don't care — you just show love in more reserved ways.
results.comment: Tips: Try to be more vulnerable sometimes. Opening up doesn't mean weakness — it means trusting the other person enough to let them in.

results.title: Anxious Attachment
results.description: Love is extremely important to you, but you're constantly worried about whether you're loved enough. You're very sensitive to your partner's every move and tend to overthink. Your love is deeply passionate, just sometimes accompanied by anxiety.
results.comment: Tips: Remember, your worth doesn't depend on your partner's response. Try to build more self-confidence, and you'll find your anxiety naturally decreases.

results.title: Fearful-Avoidant Attachment
results.description: You deeply desire intimacy but are simultaneously afraid of getting hurt. This makes you swing between craving closeness and pushing people away. Your emotional landscape is rich and complex — you need a particularly patient partner who understands.
results.comment: Tips: Recognizing your own patterns is the first step to change. Try noticing when you're pushing someone away — is it because you're afraid, or because you truly want distance?

title: Past Life Love Connection Test
description: Was your meeting fate or coincidence? Uncover your connections from a past life.

questions.text: You arrive at a mysterious ancient temple. What catches your eye first?

options.text: A glowing warm oil lamp
Ancient texts full of faded writing
A gorgeous mural depicting celestial beings
A mysterious hovering crystal in the center

questions.text: A fortune teller at the temple says your past life was…

options.text: A loving couple who looked after each other through hardship
A scholar and their devoted reader companion
Wandering performers who traveled the world together
A monarch and their trusted royal advisor

questions.text: An old chest appears before you. What's inside?

options.text: A pair of matching jade pendants
An ancient letter sealed with wax
An exquisite hand-painted fan
A half of a mysterious amulet

questions.text: You suddenly see a scene from your past life. What is it?

options.text: Two people sitting together watching the sunset by a riverbank
Someone reading alone under a tree in the rain
A lively festival marketplace full of noise and color
A vast starry sky with two silhouettes standing on a cliff

questions.text: If you could bring one item from your past life, what would it be?

options.text: A handmade gift from your past-life lover
A diary recording shared memories
A musical instrument played at many celebrations
A ring with mysterious protective powers

questions.text: What kind of promise do you think was made between you in a past life?

options.text: "Let's be together in the next life, just like now"
"Even if we can't be together, I hope we meet again"
"No matter where, I'll find you"
"Even across lifetimes, our souls will recognize each other"

questions.text: In your past life, what was the most important moment between you two?

options.text: Holding each other through a difficult time
A night of deep conversation that connected your hearts
Creating a beautiful work of art or music together
Making a life-changing decision together

questions.text: The fortune teller says your biggest challenge in this life's love will be…

options.text: Learning to let go and not hold too tight
Daring to speak your true feelings
Finding someone who matches your free spirit
Building trust after being hurt before

questions.text: As you leave the temple, a mysterious voice says to you…

options.text: "The person you're looking for is already by your side"
"True connection needs no words"
"Follow your heart; it knows the way"
"Your fate is already written in the stars"

questions.text: After leaving the temple, the emotion you feel most strongly is…

options.text: A warm sense of belonging
Peace and quiet understanding
Excitement and anticipation for what's coming
A profound feeling that everything is connected

results.title: Destined Reunited Lovers
results.description: You and your person were deeply in love in a past life, and fate has brought you together again in this one. Your love transcends time — you feel an inexplicable sense of familiarity and warmth. Your mission in this life is to complete the unfinished love story.
results.comment: Tips: This deep connection is beautiful, but remember to also build your present-life self. The best relationships are two complete people choosing each other.

results.title: Soulmate Across Time
results.description: In a past life, you were close but not romantically involved — perhaps friends or mentors. In this life, this connection has evolved into a deeper bond. Your relationship is special because it's built on understanding rather than just attraction.
results.comment: Tips: Cherish this unique bond. Don't compare your relationship to conventional romance — your connection is on a deeper, rarer level.

results.title: Free Spirits United
results.description: In a past life, you were both free-spirited adventurers. Your souls crossed paths at markets, festivals, and distant lands. In this life, you'll find each other through shared enthusiasm for life, art, and adventure. Your love is colorful and ever-changing.
results.comment: Tips: While exploring life together, remember to build a stable foundation. The best adventures have a home base to return to.

results.title: Star-Crossed Destiny
results.description: Your past-life connection was profound and fateful — perhaps a grand love story separated by circumstance. In this life, the universe conspires to bring you together again. When you meet, you'll feel an overwhelming sense of recognition that defies logic.
results.comment: Tips: This intense connection can be overwhelming. Take your time — let love unfold naturally rather than trying to rush destiny.

title: Love Instruction Manual
description: This is your personalized Love Instruction Manual. Through 15 everyday scenarios, we'll analyze your love language, conflict style, and ideal partner type.

questions.text: On a weekend morning, what's your ideal scenario with your partner?

options.text: Making breakfast together, chatting about plans for the day
Cuddling in bed, scrolling phones together
Each doing their own thing, occasionally exchanging smiles
Going out separately, meeting up later for dinner

questions.text: Your partner sends you a long, heartfelt message. Your response?

options.text: Immediately call them — text can't express what you feel
Carefully type an equally long reply
Send a heart emoji and say 'me too'
Feel a bit overwhelmed, reply briefly after thinking

questions.text: How would you prefer to celebrate an anniversary?

options.text: A romantic candlelit dinner for two
Travel to a place you've always wanted to visit
Cozy movie night at home
A simple acknowledgment — fancy celebrations feel forced

questions.text: You're upset about something at work. What do you want from your partner?

options.text: A big hug and words of comfort
Listen patiently without giving advice
Help analyze the problem and find solutions
Give me space — I need to process alone first

questions.text: You notice your partner is in a bad mood. What do you do?

options.text: Ask directly what happened and offer help
Quietly sit beside them, letting them know you're there
Prepare their favorite food or drink
Give them space and check in later

questions.text: Which love language means the most to you?

options.text: Words of affirmation — hearing 'I love you' daily
Physical touch — holding hands, hugging
Acts of service — they do things for you
Quality time — undivided attention together

questions.text: After an argument, who usually initiates reconciliation?

options.text: I always go first — can't stand being in a fight

I wait a bit, but will eventually make the first move

questions.text: What would really disappoint you in a partner?

Being too clingy and controlling

questions.text: At a party, your partner talks to an attractive stranger for a long time. Your reaction?

options.text: Walk over and naturally join the conversation
Trust them completely, continue talking to your own friends
Feel a bit uneasy, keep glancing over
Bring it up tactfully afterwards

questions.text: Do you think couples should share phone passwords?

options.text: Of course! Nothing to hide from each other
It's fine either way, depends on the couple
I'd rather not — everyone deserves privacy

questions.text: What's your ideal frequency of dates?

options.text: Every day if possible — can't get enough
3-4 times a week is perfect
Once or twice a week — quality over quantity
A few times a month is enough

questions.text: If your partner wanted to change careers into something risky, you'd…

options.text: Fully support them — dreams are worth chasing
Support but suggest practical planning first
Express concern and hope they think carefully
Honestly share your worries about financial stability

questions.text: How important is meeting each other's families?

options.text: Very important — family approval matters a lot
Important, but it's our relationship that matters most
Not essential — as long as we're happy
We can deal with it when the time comes

questions.text: If you could describe your ideal relationship in one word, it'd be…

options.text: Warmth
Growth
Freedom
Balance

questions.text: What do you think is the secret to making love last?

options.text: Never stop expressing love and appreciation
Growing and evolving together
Respecting each other's individuality
Honest communication, even when it's hard

results.title: The Independent Thinker
results.description: You value personal space and rational thinking in love. You believe the best relationships are two complete people choosing each other, not two halves desperately seeking completion. Your love style is measured and thoughtful.
results.comment: Tips: Your independence is admirable, but don't forget to occasionally let your guard down and let your partner in. Vulnerability is also a form of strength.

results.title: The Steady Partner
results.description: You approach love with a balanced and grounded perspective. Neither too clingy nor too distant, you know how to give space while staying connected. You're the type of partner who builds lasting relationships through consistent, reliable affection.
results.comment: Tips: Your balanced approach is a real strength. Try adding more spontaneous romantic gestures occasionally — they can bring fresh energy to a stable relationship.

results.title: The Passionate Devotee
results.description: Love is central to your life. You express feelings openly, crave closeness, and pour your heart into relationships. Your love style is warm, attentive, and deeply caring — your partner always knows how much they mean to you.
results.comment: Tips: Your warmth is beautiful, but remember that sometimes the best way to love is to give your partner room to miss you. A healthy balance keeps the spark alive.

results.title: The All-In Romantic
results.description: You love with your whole heart, holding nothing back. Romance, passion, and emotional connection are your oxygen. You believe love should be celebrated loudly and often — and you're not afraid to show it.
results.comment: Tips: Your intensity is beautiful, but be careful not to overwhelm your partner. The best love stories also include chapters where each person shines on their own.

title: Crush Radar: Your Lovesick Index
description: Everyone says you fall too hard too fast? Through 10 everyday scenarios, find out how susceptible you are to catching feelings.

questions.text: Someone you just met sends you a 'Good morning' text the next day. Your reaction?

options.text: That's nice, reply politely and move on
Smile a little, think 'they remembered me'
Heart skips a beat, start crafting the perfect reply
Already imagining what your future dates will look like

questions.text: You see someone attractive on the subway making eye contact. What happens in your head?

options.text: Nothing much, go back to scrolling your phone
Quick internal note: 'Oh, cute.' Then move on
Start a whole mental storyline about running into them again
Consider getting off at the same stop to 'accidentally' bump into them

questions.text: Someone you like sends you a song. What do you do?

options.text: Listen once, reply 'Nice song'
Look up the lyrics to see if there's a hidden message
Put it on repeat and analyze every line
Already making it 'our song' in your head

questions.text: Your crush accidentally touches your hand. Your internal reaction?

options.text: Notice it, but stay cool
Feel a little spark, but play it off
Heart rate goes up, replay the moment for hours
That one touch keeps you awake at night

questions.text: How often do you check if your crush is online?

options.text: Never; that's not my style
Once in a while, if I happen to be on the app
Check their last-seen status throughout the day
You practically have their online schedule memorized

questions.text: You overhear someone mention your crush's name. Your reaction?

options.text: Ears perk up slightly, but you stay cool
Casually tune in to see what they're saying
Immediately laser-focus on the conversation
Can't help but join in: 'Wait, what about them?!'

questions.text: Your crush posts a photo on social media. What do you do?

options.text: Scroll past; I don't react to every post
Like it casually, move on
Like it, zoom in, analyze the background and who's in it
Screenshot, analyze, then debate whether to comment

questions.text: You're about to meet your crush. How much time do you spend getting ready?

options.text: Normal routine, same as meeting anyone
A few extra minutes to look presentable
Change outfits at least twice, check the mirror ten times
Full fashion show at home, ask friends to vote on the best look

questions.text: Your friend says your crush might like someone else. Your reaction?

options.text: Oh well, that's life. Move on
A little disappointed, but you'll be okay
Mood drops, you need ice cream and sad music tonight
Entire world collapses. You start an investigation immediately

questions.text: If you could describe your current state of falling for someone, it'd be…

options.text: I'm firmly on dry land. Completely stable
Gently swaying in a boat, enjoying the breeze
The boat is rocking and I'm getting dizzy
I've already capsized and I'm drowning happily

results.title: 【Ironclad】 Emotionally Bulletproof
results.description: Your heart is like a fortress — nothing gets in easily. You approach feelings with extreme rationality and self-control. It takes a LOT to even make you blink romantically.
results.comment: Tips: Being self-sufficient is great, but don't forget that letting someone past your walls can be one of life's greatest adventures. Consider opening the gate once in a while.

results.title: 【Gentle Breeze】Light Crush Alert
results.description: You feel the occasional flutter but always have your feet on the ground. You can appreciate someone's charm without losing your composure. Your romantic antennae are working, but you're not overwhelmed by signals.
results.comment: Tips: Your balanced approach is healthy. Just make sure you don't overthink things to the point of missing genuine connections. Sometimes it's okay to follow the flutter.

results.title: 【Seasick】Moderate Lovesickness
results.description: Your ship is clearly rocking! You're very susceptible to romantic gestures and small signals. When you like someone, it shows — in your behavior, your mood, and probably your search history.
results.comment: Tips: Your romantic nature is a gift! Just make sure you're reading signals correctly. Not every 'Good morning' text means 'I want to marry you.' Take a breath.

results.title: 【Sinking Ship】Hopelessly Lovesick
results.description: The lifeguard alarm is going off! You practically catch feelings the moment someone gives you the slightest sunshine. When it comes to your crush, all rationality goes overboard — you've completely and utterly capsized.
results.comment: Tips: SOS! Keep your friends on speed-dial for reality checks. Love yourself first before diving in — don't lose yourself in someone else's waters!

title: Do We Have a Future Together?
description: 15 situational questions to deeply analyze your compatibility, communication chemistry, and future potential — revealing where this relationship might go.

questions.text: You planned to meet on the weekend, but she suddenly says she needs to reschedule. Your first reaction?

options.text: No problem — proactively suggest another time
A bit disappointed, but you say it's fine
Feel uncomfortable inside, but won't say it
Feel like she doesn't value your plans

questions.text: When you two chat, how do conversations usually go?

options.text: Can't stop talking, often chatting until dawn
A few reliable topics, occasionally sparks fly
Mostly you initiating, she doesn't respond much

results.title: A Mutual Sunshine

results.title: A Once-in-a-Lifetime Aurora

results.title: 【Glacier Type】 The Zen Observer

results.title: 【Temperate Type】The Cautious Observer

results.title: 【Subtropical Type】The Easygoing Balancer

results.title: Love Brain 30%: The Flexible Warrior

results.title: Balanced Mode: The Smart Battery
results.description: Your social battery is medium-sized and auto-adjusts its output to the situation and company. You can get hyped with people you know, but you switch to power-saving mode in unfamiliar settings. You've got the social-versus-solo balance figured out.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You have excellent social flexibility. Keep up this well-judged rhythm, it lets you move through any relationship with ease.

results.title: Fast Charge: The Efficient Power Bank
results.description: You've got solid social endurance and you bounce back fast. Sure, you get tired sometimes, but a little rest or a chat with someone you vibe with, and you're back above 80% in no time.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You're the reliable ray of sunshine in your friend group, but don't forget to listen to your own inner voice too. A full day switched off does wonders for body and mind.

results.title: Infinite Mode: The Nuclear Reactor
results.description: Incredible! Your social energy is basically limitless, and the livelier the scene, the more it fires you up. You don't need alone time to recharge. Interacting with people and soaking up the crowd's energy IS your best charging method.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You're a natural social pro and the ultimate vibe-setter, but be careful not to accidentally drain all those 'solar cell' friends of yours!

title: Your Inner Villain Rating
description: Uncover the hidden 'dark side' lurking within, and your true villain charisma!

questions.text: You catch someone talking trash about you behind your back. You:

options.text: Confront them face-to-face. Not afraid to blow it up.
Stay calm on the surface, but quietly start collecting dirt on them.
Feel hurt, and go cry it out to a close friend.
Don't care at all. They're too beneath me to be worth my anger.

questions.text: When playing competitive games (board or mobile), your style is:

options.text: I'll do anything to win, even ruthlessly betray a friend.
I love pulling strings behind the scenes and letting others take each other out.
Zen player. Winning doesn't matter, as long as it's fun.
Losing kills me. I demand a rematch immediately until I win.

questions.text: If you suddenly got the power of invisibility, the first thing you'd do is:

options.text: Sneak into fancy or off-limits places I could never normally enter.
Go eavesdrop on what the people I dislike say in private.
Prank and scare my friends just to watch them freak out.
Hide somewhere quiet and nap in total, undisturbed peace.

questions.text: When someone you dislike fails or embarrasses themselves, your inner monologue is:

options.text: I burst out laughing, almost want to applaud: 'So you DO have off days!'
A little smirk, quietly thinking 'serves you right.'
I dislike them, but seeing them this miserable, I feel a tiny bit sorry.
Nothing at all. Their business has nothing to do with me.

questions.text: What do you think is the real rule this world runs on?

options.text: Survival of the fittest. Only the strong get to make the rules.
Trading favors. No permanent friends, only permanent interests.
Social connections. Play the relationships right and anything gets solved.
Luck decides everything. Hard work matters less than being born rich.

questions.text: You find a wallet stuffed with cash on the street. No cameras, no ID inside. You:

options.text: Keep it without hesitation. This is the luck I deserve.
Take the cash and toss the empty wallet in the trash to destroy evidence.
After a serious internal battle, decide to turn it in to the police.
Don't even touch it. Walk right by, afraid of getting into trouble.

questions.text: When someone asks you for a favor, your usual attitude is:

options.text: Weigh what's in it for me. I only help if there's a condition.
Help if it's easy, firmly decline if it's a hassle.
I struggle to say no and often force myself to agree.
Pretend I didn't hear, or brush it off with an excuse.

questions.text: Which of the 'seven deadly sins' do you commit most often?

options.text: Pride (I think I'm smarter and better than most people).
Envy (I can't stand others doing better than me, I get jealous easily).
Wrath (My temper takes over. I get emotional and can't control it).
Sloth (Lie down over sit down. I absolutely loathe hassle).

questions.text: If you were the villain in a drama, what ending would you want?

options.text: Go out in a blaze of glory, so no one ever forgets me.
Fool everyone, then vanish with a fortune.
Redeem myself at the last second, sacrificing everything to protect someone I love.
Get defeated and thrown in prison, but flash an eerie smile in the final shot.

questions.text: What do the words 'being kind' mean in modern society, to you?

options.text: A fig leaf for the weak. A synonym for pushover.
A choice, but it needs an edge or it gets exploited.
The most precious human quality. The world needs more warmth.
A disguise. Nobody is truly selflessly kind.

results.title: The Harmless Bunny
results.description: Your heart is incredibly soft, with almost no villain traits. Even when people treat you badly, you often choose to forgive or step back. You believe the world is still a beautiful place, and kindness is your most precious weapon.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Kindness is a virtue, but grow yourself a little edge too, so you don't end up the pushover everyone takes advantage of.

results.title: The Chaotic-Good Phantom Thief
results.description: You've got a mischievous, rebellious streak and you won't blindly follow rules. You carry your own moral compass and might pull a little stunt now and then for fun or self-protection, but your line is crystal clear. You'd never do real harm.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You know how to protect yourself in the real world, and that shades-of-gray quality makes you irresistibly charming.

results.title: The Smiling Tiger
results.description: You've got deep cunning and a cool, calculating mind. On the surface you're friendly, even a little zen, but you actually see the whole board clearly. Cross your line and you'll deliver a fatal blow from the shadows.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Silent until you strike, then you stun everyone. You have powerful strategic thinking, so remember to point that brilliance in the right direction.

results.title: The Supreme Overlord
results.description: Your villain stats are off the charts! You're wildly ambitious, willing to do whatever it takes, with a strong streak of control and pride. In the movie, you're absolutely that charismatic, wildly popular final boss who puts the heroes through hell.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your presence is powerful, with natural dominance and leadership. Just keep your temper and your need for control in check, so you don't actually become everyone's target.

title: Your Workplace Survival Instinct
description: Which animal are you in the office jungle? Test your stress tolerance and adaptability.

questions.text: First day at a new company. The first thing you do is:

options.text: Introduce myself to every department to build my network fast.
Quietly watch how coworkers interact and figure out who's really in charge.
Focus on learning my own job. Not interested in other people's business.
Find a friendly-looking coworker and quietly dig for gossip and landmines.

questions.text: In a meeting, your boss proposes a clearly flawed decision. You:

options.text: Raise my hand right there, question it, and offer a better plan.
Agree on the surface, then subtly hint to my boss later in private.
Pretend I didn't notice. The tall folks hold up the sky, I just do as told.
Watch how the senior coworkers react and follow the wind, never sticking my neck out.

questions.text: A coworker dumps their mess on you. Your reaction is:

options.text: Flatly refuse. Boundaries locked in tight. I don't take the loss.
Annoyed, but I quietly take it and grind through the overtime myself.
Agree on the surface, then do it badly on purpose so they won't ask again.
Take it with a smile, then trade a favor to get an intern or coworker to do it.

questions.text: When you notice cliques forming at work, you:

options.text: Become the core of one of them and hold the influence.
Stay neutral, friendly with everyone on the surface, but close to no one.
Find it exhausting. Focus on work and stay out of the palace intrigue.
Bend like grass in the wind, leaning toward whoever has the advantage.

questions.text: Facing the same boring, repetitive work every day, your mindset is:

options.text: Actively look for ways to optimize the process and make boring fun.
Not thrilled, but I do it well and thoroughly for the paycheck.
Soul out of body, going through the motions while my brain plans dinner.
Start secretly online-shopping or working my side hustle on the clock.

questions.text: It's clocking-out time, but your boss hasn't left. You:

options.text: Clock out right on time. Off-hours are my sacred, untouchable territory.
Politely ask if the boss needs help. I only leave once they say no.
Look busy for a bit, then leave once another coworker goes first.
Stay and do unpaid overtime just to look dedicated to the boss.

questions.text: If the company had layoffs, you think you'd be:

options.text: On the safe list for sure. I hold the core tech or key clients.
A bit at risk. I'm obedient, but my performance isn't the standout.
Don't care. I wanted to quit anyway, this way I get severance.
Actively fishing for intel, updating my resume early to jump ship.

questions.text: During the year-end review, how do you usually rate your own performance?

options.text: Sell my achievements hard, turning a 60 into a 100.
Fairly and objectively list my strengths and weaknesses, plus next year's plan.
Way too humble. I did a lot but don't dare write it down.
Copy-paste last year's content and call it a day.

questions.text: The company organizes a staff trip. Your honest thoughts?

options.text: Awesome! A chance to bond with coworkers from other departments.
I'll play along with everyone, a nice change of pace to unwind.
Total misery. A trip with coworkers is just overtime in a new location.
Invent every absurd excuse to skip it. I'd rather sleep at home.

questions.text: For you, what's the ultimate point of work?

options.text: Self-fulfillment, chasing achievement and social status.
A stable income. As long as it maintains a decent quality of life.
Just a tool for making money. I don't want to invest any feelings in it.
A training ground for making friends and learning new skills.

results.title: The Quiet, Honest Sloth
results.description: You keep out of workplace conflict and just want to get your own tasks done in peace. You hate complicated office politics and competition, and pressure makes you anxious. You're obedient, but that also makes you an easy scapegoat or the one left out.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Show your abilities and your limits a little. Don't let your kindness become the reason people push you around.

results.title: The Clever, Slick Fox
results.description: You've got sharp workplace instincts. You read the room and protect yourself well. You never start trouble, but you always dodge crises gracefully. You're a master of keeping the peace and a survivor who won't get cut easily.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your cleverness saves you a lot of detours, but if you channeled it into creating real value, you'd feel far more fulfilled.

results.title: The Steady, Loyal Dog
results.description: You're the backbone of the company, responsible and dependable. You communicate well, you finish what your boss assigns, and you work seamlessly with coworkers. You may not always be the flashiest one, but you're absolutely indispensable.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your ability is plain for all to see. Try fighting for the resources and spotlight you deserve. Don't be so overly humble.

results.title: The Ambitious Lion
results.description: You bring powerful presence and a hunger to lead at work. You don't fear challenges. You actually crave competition, because it's your chance to prove yourself. Your goals are clear, and you'll show fierce execution and savvy to get there.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your ambition is your engine, but remember to soften up now and then and tend to your teammates' feelings. It'll make your climb far smoother.

title: Your Hidden Money Personality
description: Discover why you can't hold onto cash, and the wealth path you're destined for!

questions.text: On payday, how do you usually celebrate?

options.text: Treat myself to a big meal or that pricey thing I've been eyeing.
Move my savings to a separate account first, then spend what's left.
Nothing special. Life goes on, the money just sits there.
Immediately throw it into stocks or crypto and let money make money.

questions.text: You see something you love but it's a bit pricey. Your buying process is:

options.text: If I love it, why not? Just swipe the card!
Compare a few shops and wait for a sale or free shipping to strike.
Agonize forever, then usually give up because I can't bear to spend.
Ask whether it'll appreciate later, or whether it could make me money.

questions.text: A friend invites you into a high-return-sounding investment. You:

options.text: Weak-willed. I figure a friend wouldn't scam me, so I pay in right away.
Study the financials and business model. Only consider it if the risk is manageable.
Never touch what I don't understand. A bank deposit feels safest.
Test the waters with a little spare cash. If I lose it, so be it.

questions.text: If you won a million in the lottery, where does the first chunk go?

options.text: Book a flight for a luxury trip abroad and enjoy life immediately.
Pay off debts: mortgage, car loan, student loans.
Save it all or buy insurance, a safety net for future me.
Use it as startup capital and try to turn a million into ten.

questions.text: When your account hits rock bottom at month's end, your reaction is:

options.text: Panic: 'Oh my god, where did all my money even go?!'
Used to it. Time to survive on instant noodles and mooching meals.
This never happens. I keep a strict budget every single month.
Quickly grab freelance gigs or a part-time job to earn it back.

questions.text: Your attitude toward 'budgeting/tracking expenses' is:

options.text: A three-day fad. Downloaded the app, used it for three days.
I log every expense clearly, down to the last cent.
I roughly note the big spends. Small change, too lazy to bother.
Tracking is pointless. I'd rather spend that time figuring out how to earn more.

questions.text: When you hit a financial crisis, who do you turn to for help?

options.text: Ask my parents or family to bail me out.
Borrow from a trusted close friend and promise to pay it back.
Never ask anyone. I tough it out or take a bank loan.
Sell off my valuables to raise cash.

questions.text: What do you think is the key to becoming rich?

options.text: Good luck. Winning the lottery or being born into money.
Down to earth. Pinch pennies daily and slowly build wealth.
Daring to take big risks and grab the chances others won't touch.
Building passive income systems that let money work for you.

questions.text: On 'paying for time or convenience' (delivery, taxis, etc.), your view is:

options.text: Totally for it! Time is money. If cash can solve it, it's not a problem.
Fine once in a while, but doing it too often makes me feel guilty.
Absolutely against it! I'd rather walk further or cook than pay extra.
Depends on whether it's worth it relative to my hourly rate.

questions.text: Picture your retirement. What do you most want to have?

options.text: Travel the world and taste every kind of luxurious life.
Buy some land in the countryside, grow veggies, live simply and self-sufficient.
Endless money, so my only worry is how to spend it all.
Keep running the work I love. Retire but never fully stop.

results.title: The Paycheck-to-Paycheck Leaker
results.description: 'Live for today' is your motto. You don't have much of a concept of money and often spend on impulse, so your account balance is too painful to look at. Your money personality is a leaky bucket, no matter how much comes in, it drains right out.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Force yourself into a 'save first, spend later' habit and try tracking expenses for one month to find your money leaks.

results.title: The Steady Saver
results.description: Your money mindset is very conservative. You believe keeping cash in the bank or in ultra-safe options is the way to go. You won't waste money, but you also rarely see explosive growth. You're the steady type who grows old slowly and safely.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Within a risk level you can afford, try learning some investing basics so inflation doesn't eat up your hard-earned cash.

results.title: The Savvy Money Master
results.description: You have excellent control over money. You know how to budget carefully and allocate assets. You spend every dollar where it counts and let steady investing snowball your wealth over time.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your finances are super healthy! Keep up the discipline. Financial freedom is just a matter of time for you.

results.title: The Bold Cash Magnet
results.description: You've got a natural magnetic field for wealth! You dare to take risks, you have a sharp eye, and you're always hunting for the next money-making chance. To you, money is a tool to create more value. Time your moves right and you can leap up the wealth ladder easily.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: High returns come with high risk. While chasing big gains, never forget asset protection and risk management.

title: Your Love Dopamine Guide
description: In love, do you chase thrills or stability? Find out your love-brain type.

questions.text: At the start of a relationship, what kind of person draws you in most?

options.text: Stunning, magnetic, with a little dangerous edge.
Gentle and caring, someone who makes you feel totally safe.
Funny and quick-witted, always able to make you laugh.
Talented, shining in some field, someone you can admire.

questions.text: What does your idea of the perfect date look like?

options.text: No plan, just go. An adventure full of the unknown and surprise.
Fancy restaurant and spots booked ahead, everything running perfectly.
Both of us curled up at home ordering takeout and gaming. Easy and cozy.
Hit an exhibit or a talk, learn something new and swap ideas.

questions.text: When your partner argues with you, how do you want them to handle it?

options.text: Hug me and apologize right away, sweet-talk me back to happy.
Both of us cool off, then talk it through rationally afterward.
Buy my favorite food or a gift as an apology.
Proactively analyze why we fought and propose a concrete fix.

questions.text: How often do you need your partner to express love (say 'I love you' or hug you)?

options.text: Every single second! I'm a puppy who craves touch and reassurance.
At least once a day. There has to be a ritual before bed or heading out.
Let it flow naturally. Too much on purpose feels cheesy and awkward.
No need to say it. Just let me feel it through your actions.

questions.text: You find out your partner went out to eat one-on-one with a friend of the opposite sex. Your reaction:

options.text: Jealousy overflows! Call immediately to check in, maybe demand a video call.
A little bothered inside, but play it cool and ask once they're back.
Fully trust them. As long as they gave me a heads up, it's fine.
Tit for tat. Next day I go out with a friend of the opposite sex to spite them.

questions.text: What's the one dealbreaker you can't tolerate in a relationship?

options.text: Lying and betrayal. One time is unforgivable.
No plan for the future, just drifting along.
Too controlling, meddling in my friendships and personal space.
No sense of occasion, forgetting even anniversaries and birthdays.

questions.text: What do you think you most lack in a relationship?

options.text: Security. I always feel they'll leave me one day.
Freshness. Once the honeymoon ends, I get bored and want to quit.
Boundaries. I often lose myself trying to please my partner.
Empathy. Sometimes I'm too rational and miss their feelings.

questions.text: Your partner prepares a surprise. What do you most hope to get?

options.text: An elaborate romantic display, ideally something to show off to friends.
A handwritten letter or a custom gift full of our shared memories.
A straight-up money transfer, or that pricey useful thing I never bought.
An all-inclusive couples trip where I don't have to plan a thing.

questions.text: When the relationship hits a plateau, how do you keep the heat up?

options.text: Manufacture a little drama or jealousy to keep them on their toes.
Set a regular date night, put the phones down, and focus on each other.
Let it flow. The quiet of an old married couple is its own happiness.
Try new things we've never done together (bungee jumping, a craft class).

questions.text: For you, what should love's final destination be?

options.text: Love passionately while it lasts, no need to force an outcome.
Walk into marriage and build a warm, stable little family.
Become each other's strongest backup, leveling up together in life and work.
Like best friends who tell each other everything, growing old side by side.

results.title: The Security-First Sloth
results.description: In love, you crave absolute stability and the slow burn. You don't need over-the-top romance, just daily good-mornings and good-nights and real, steady company. To you, quiet happiness is the truest kind.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Stability is great, but sprinkle in a little surprise now and then to keep the love fresh!

results.title: The Level-Headed Owl
results.description: You're a very rational lover, not easily swept away by a moment's passion. You value mental connection and long-term compatibility. More than sweet words, you value your partner's ability to solve problems and plan a future.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: In love, it's okay to put the brain aside sometimes and feel your partner's emotions and affection with your heart.

results.title: The Ceremony-Loving Peacock
results.description: You truly savor the romantic atmosphere of love, and grand gestures are non-negotiable for you! You love feeling cherished and adored, and you'll happily prepare surprises for your partner too. Your love is bursting with pink bubbles.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Romance is wonderful, but learn to accept that love inevitably cycles into calmer phases. That's the real test.

results.title: The Thrill-Chasing Wild Horse
results.description: You're a hopeless romantic and a thrill-seeker! You love that heart-racing, rollercoaster feeling of falling in love. The moment things go quiet and dull, you lose interest. You love fiercely, but dangerously.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Dopamine always fades. Try finding ways to build a deep soul connection after the passion, so you're not forever chasing the next target.

title: Your Past-Life Soul & This Life's Lesson
description: With a touch of the mystical, explore your soul's deepest skill points and unfinished ambitions.

questions.text: If you could pick one antique, which would you choose?

options.text: An ornate dagger stained with old, dried blood.
A yellowed parchment book covered in symbols you can't read.
An exquisitely carved brass pocket watch full of hidden mechanisms.
A pure gold crown set with an enormous gem.

questions.text: What kind of dreams do you have most often?

options.text: Thrilling ones: always running, fighting, or falling from a height.
Dreams where I can fly or wield magic.
An ancient city I've never visited but feels strangely familiar.
Standing on high, looking down over a crowd, admired by all.

questions.text: Walking into an old castle, where does your eye go first?

options.text: The castle's defenses and where the armory is.
The study or cellar hiding a secret passage.
The family portraits of generations hanging on the walls.
The grand banquet hall and the throne.

questions.text: If you could have one supernatural power, you'd want:

options.text: An indestructible body and immense strength.
The ability to see the future or read minds.
Teleporting instantly to any corner of the world.
Control over the natural elements (wind, fire, water, earth).

questions.text: In an unfamiliar place, what do you rely on to judge if it's safe?

options.text: Scanning the terrain and mapping escape routes.
My sixth sense. If it feels off, I retreat immediately.
Chatting up the locals to gather intel.
Reading the expressions and subtle movements of people around me.

questions.text: What historical era or culture especially fascinates you?

options.text: War-torn times like the Three Kingdoms or the age of medieval knights.
The mystery-shrouded ancient Egypt or Maya civilization.
The romantic, thriving Renaissance or Age of Exploration.
The golden glory of the Roman Empire or the Tang Dynasty.

questions.text: What difficulty do you feel you face most often in this life?

options.text: A hot temper. I clash with people easily.
Being too sensitive. I soak up other people's negativity.
Can't sit still. Always feel there's an endless to-do list waiting.
Can't lower my guard. I care too much about others' opinions and my pride.

questions.text: What kind of aura do friends often say you carry?

options.text: A powerful presence, a natural authority without even trying.
An ethereal, otherworldly air, like you're above it all.
Socially deft, able to chat with anyone, warm and approachable.
Calm and reserved, an old soul who sees through the world.

questions.text: Lost in the wilderness, which way does instinct pull you?

options.text: Climb high to find a vantage point and read the terrain.
Close my eyes and follow my gut and the guidance of the wind.
Find water and follow the river downstream.
Stay put, build a fire, and wait for rescue.

questions.text: What do you think the purpose of soul reincarnation is?

options.text: To make up for the regrets and unfinished ambitions of a past life.
To experience different lives and learn the lessons the soul is meant to learn.
To pay off the karma and debts of love accumulated across lifetimes.
Reincarnation is just a random process, no special purpose at all.

results.title: The Ruler / Noble
results.description: Deep in your soul is imprinted the memory of power and duty. In a past life you may have been a leader or noble carrying heavy responsibility. This life, you still carry an uncommon air and pride. Your lesson is to learn to release your need for control and treat those around you more gently.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: True strength isn't making others fear you. It's making them respect you from the heart.

results.title: The Artisan / Chronicler
results.description: In a past life you may have been a craftsman devoted to a trade, or a scholar recording history. You have a delicate mind and a fierce dedication to your craft. This life, you still love diving deep into what interests you. Your lesson is to learn to express your feelings, not always hide your thoughts away.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You have hands and a heart that can create beauty. Try sharing your talent with the world more.

results.title: The Mystic / Priest
results.description: You carry an unusually strong spiritual nature and intuition. In a past life you may have been a priest connected to the divine, or a healer skilled with herbs. This life, you resonate easily with others' emotions. Your lesson is to build emotional boundaries and protect your energy from being drained.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your intuition is remarkably accurate. Trust your inner voice. It's your soul's compass.

results.title: The Warrior / Adventurer
results.description: Your soul is full of wildness and a hunger for battle. In a past life you may have been a general charging across the battlefield or a wandering explorer. This life, you still love a challenge and hate being tied down. Your lesson is to quiet the restlessness within and find your own inner peace.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Brave is your middle name, but don't forget: pausing to enjoy the view now and then is a practice too.

title: Your Late-Stage Procrastination Chart
description: Straight for the sore spot! Find out why you always leave things to the last second. Which terminal procrastinator are you?

questions.text: One week before the deadline, your progress is usually:

options.text: Already 80% done, ready to wrap up the final touches.
Just created a blank file named 'New Folder.'
Rehearsed the perfect draft in my head a hundred times, but haven't lifted a finger.
What deadline? I completely forgot this was even a thing!

questions.text: The moment you decide to 'start working,' what do you usually do first?

options.text: Write a detailed to-do list and follow it step by step.
Decide my desk is too messy and launch into a full room clean.
Scroll my phone for ten minutes to relax, then an hour has vanished.
Go make a coffee or grab a snack. The prep takes longer than the work.

questions.text: What do you think is the main reason you procrastinate?

options.text: Perfectionism. Things aren't good enough yet, so I can't start.
It feels too hard and complex. I don't know where to begin.
Pure laziness. If I can lie down, I will never sit up.
I love that last-second adrenaline rush of clutch play.

questions.text: After finally finishing something you'd put off forever, you feel:

options.text: Full of pride. I'm honestly a genius!
Drained and wrecked. Swear I'll never procrastinate again (but I will).
The result is bad. Should have just started earlier.
Relieved. Immediately open a game or binge shows in revenge.

questions.text: When you realize you're about to run out of time, what happens to you?

options.text: Eerily calm. Focus skyrockets 500%, entering a state of pure flow.
Anxious to the point of stomachache, crying while I type through the crunch.
Start inventing absurd reasons to ask my boss or teacher for an extension.
Give up treatment. Throw up my hands and decide to just phone it in.

questions.text: What's your go-to method for 'escaping reality'?

options.text: Binge mindless short videos or garbage posts like crazy.
Suddenly start googling random trivia (like how many feathers a penguin has).
Go to sleep, hypnotizing myself that I'll have inspiration when I wake.
Panic-shop online, numbing the anxiety by spending money.

questions.text: Your follow-through rate on a plan you've made is usually:

options.text: Over 90%. I'm an extremely self-disciplined robot.
About 50%. Plans can't keep up with change.
10%. A schedule is just a pretty piece of scrap paper to me.
I never make plans. I just go on feel.

questions.text: If someone nags you to hand something in, your reaction is:

options.text: Honestly report my progress and give an exact finish time.
Say 'almost done, almost done' when I actually haven't started.
Get irritated. The more you push me, the less I want to do it.
Pretend I didn't see the message and go into vanish mode.

questions.text: What kind of environment helps you focus most?

options.text: Dead silent, where you could hear a pin drop.
A cafe or library with a little white noise.
Once the deadline's near, I could focus in the front row of a concert.
Environment doesn't really matter. It's all about my mood.

questions.text: To convince yourself to do something later, what excuse do you use?

options.text: 'I'm not in the zone right now. I'll be more efficient later.'
'One episode as a reward first, then I'll start right after.'
'There's still plenty of time. Tomorrow works just fine.'
'This isn't actually that urgent. Let me do something else first!'

results.title: The God of Efficiency
results.description: Congrats! You basically have zero procrastination. You're organized and have iron control over your time. To you, finishing early is the only way to rest easy. You're the eternal productivity legend in your friends' eyes.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your efficiency is great, but don't push yourself too hard. Letting yourself slack off once in a while is healthy too.

results.title: The Perfectionist Procrastinator
results.description: You procrastinate because you want to do things perfectly. You've dreamed up countless flawless plans in your head, but you don't dare put pen to paper, terrified the result won't match. You're not lazy. You're afraid of not being good enough.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Done beats perfect. Try getting something out first, then refining it slowly. You'll find it's not as hard as you imagined.

results.title: The Deadline-Dodger
results.description: The moment you hit stress or a hard task, you want to flee. You'll do every unrelated little chore (like cleaning your room) to numb yourself. Until the very last second, your inner adrenaline simply refuses to fire.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Break the big task into five-minute mini-steps and tell yourself 'I'll only do five minutes.' That's often enough to get rolling.

results.title: The Happily-Given-Up Procrastinator
results.description: You've reached procrastination's final form: giving up treatment. 'It'll all work out somehow' is your gospel. Even with the deadline right in front of you, you can happily binge shows and game. Your mental fortitude is unshakable. The sky could fall and you wouldn't care.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: A laid-back attitude is nice, but be careful. This let-it-rot approach can hurt other people's interests, or your own future!

title: Your Digital Detox & Phone Addiction Index
description: So relatable! Test how hooked you are on your phone and social media. Has your brain been hijacked by short videos?

questions.text: The first thing you do when you open your eyes in the morning is:

options.text: Stretch, drink some water, and start the new day.
Grab the phone with eyes still shut to kill the alarm and check the time.
Pick up the phone and scroll every social app before I'll get up.
Open my mobile games to do daily quests and grab login rewards.

questions.text: If you left home and realized you forgot your phone, your reaction is:

options.text: No big deal. A perfect day free of interruptions.
As long as I don't need to reach anyone important, I won't bother going back.
A little anxious and uneasy, like a part of my body is missing.
Even if I'm about to be late, I absolutely sprint home for it!

questions.text: During meals, where's your phone usually?

options.text: In my bag or pocket, focusing on the food.
On the table face-down. Only check it if it buzzes.
Right by my hand on standby, watching short videos or shows while I eat.
The phone 'eats' first. No chopsticks until I've shot the perfect story.

questions.text: You see an unread red dot on a social app. You:

options.text: Feel nothing. Hundreds of unread notifications, no problem.
Only open it when I have a spare moment.
OCD flares up. I must clear that red dot immediately.
Even without a red dot, I unconsciously keep pull-to-refreshing.

questions.text: In the last hour before bed, what are you usually doing?

options.text: Reading, meditating, or listening to soft music to fall asleep.
Chatting with family or partner, or prepping tomorrow's things.
Lying in bed scrolling, accidentally scrolling till the early hours.
Watching horror/thrillers or playing intense games. The later, the more hyped.

questions.text: If you vacationed somewhere with zero internet, how would you feel?

options.text: Amazing! THIS is a real vacation.
The first two days felt odd, then I slowly adjusted.
Bored out of my mind, with no idea how to pass the time.
Break down crying, hunt for signal everywhere, even want to cut the trip short.

questions.text: How often do you post your own updates on social media?

options.text: Almost never. My account looks fake, I just lurk.
Once every few months for a big event or a special trip photo.
At least one story a day, sharing life's little moments.
Shoot wherever I go. A day without posting feels wrong all over.

questions.text: When you see someone post glamorous, polished life photos, you feel:

options.text: Happy for them. Tap like and scroll on.
Nothing much. I know that's just social-media packaging.
Can't help comparing myself. Feel a bit inferior or anxious.
They're showing off. Secretly sour grapes inside.

questions.text: When you hit something you don't know, your first instinct is:

options.text: Think it through myself or go find a book at the library.
Ask an experienced friend or elder around me.
Immediately whip out the phone to Google it or ask an AI.
Post on social media and lazily ask the netizens to spoon-feed me.

questions.text: What is your phone to you?

options.text: Just a tool for handling communication.
An indispensable little helper for daily convenience.
An emotional anchor. Without it I'd lose my sense of security.
The evil digital heroin that controls my brain and my time.

results.title: Caveman Tier
results.description: Congrats! You show zero signs of internet addiction. You prefer the warmth of the real world, and the phone really is just a tool to you. You don't get hijacked by social-media information anxiety, and your real-life quality is sky-high.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Keep up this enviable state, but occasionally make sure you don't fall too far out of touch with modern info!

results.title: In-Control Tier
results.description: You've found a healthy balance between the virtual and the real. You use the internet for info and fun, but you won't let it disrupt your normal life or sleep. When you need to focus, you can decisively put the phone down.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You have excellent self-control, a rare clear-headed one in modern society. Keep up this healthy digital habit.

results.title: Mildly Hooked Tier
results.description: You're getting a bit inseparable from the internet! The moment you have a free second, your hand instinctively swipes open a social app. It's not wrecking your work yet, but your attention and sleep are slowly getting eaten by your phone.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Set a daily 'phone-free window' (say, the hour before bed) to give your eyes and brain a proper rest.

results.title: Late-Stage Addiction
results.description: Alarm bells! Your soul has been kidnapped by your phone. No internet makes you intensely anxious, even with withdrawal symptoms. Your joy and sadness are heavily swayed by social media, and you're seriously neglecting real life.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You need a full digital detox! Try starting with a half-day out on the weekend, phone left behind, to find the feel of real life again.

title: Your Pressure-Cooker Explosion Index
description: Measure how tense your body and mind are lately, spot the warning signs before you crack, and find the stress relief that fits you best.

questions.text: In the past month, which physical symptom shows up most for you?

options.text: Terrible sleep, either insomnia or restless, dream-filled nights.
Stiff neck and shoulders, migraines, or stomach and gut trouble.
Easily binge-eating, or no appetite at all.
No real issues. Eating well, sleeping well, strong as an ox.

questions.text: When something bad hits out of nowhere, your first reaction is:

options.text: Mind goes blank. No idea what to do.
Emotions instantly explode. Want to pick a fight or throw something.
Take a deep breath, calm down, and think of a solution.
Figure it's fate and reluctantly accept reality.

questions.text: Lying in bed at night about to sleep, what's on your mind?

options.text: Replaying the day's embarrassing or upsetting moments on loop.
Anxious about how much work or hassle tomorrow holds.
Weaving unrealistic daydreams, like winning the lottery.
Nothing at all. Head hits the pillow, out cold in three seconds.

questions.text: When a friend asks 'how have you been?', how do you usually answer?

options.text: 'Eh, you know, dog-tired as always.'
Smile and say 'not bad,' while it's actually bitter inside.
Seize the chance to unload and complain nonstop.
'Great! Something fun just happened recently...'

questions.text: What's the biggest source of stress in your life right now?

options.text: Office culture or the never-ending pile of work.
Money troubles, the anxiety of never having enough.
Friction in relationships, or emotional blackmail from family or partner.
Uncertainty about the future, not knowing what I even want.

questions.text: When you feel about to break down, what do you most want to do?

options.text: Lock myself in my room and cry it all out.
Book a flight to somewhere no one knows me and escape reality.
Get wasted with a good friend or scream it out at karaoke.
Devour high-calorie junk food, numbing myself with sugar.

questions.text: When you have a free day with no plans, you feel:

options.text: Guilty, like I wasted precious time.
A little bored, endlessly scrolling to see what others are up to.
Amazing! I just want to be a puddle in bed all day.
Seize the time to better myself, read or learn a new skill.

questions.text: Seeing a desk piled with unhandled clutter, how do you feel?

options.text: Frustrated and scattered. It's a snapshot of my life right now.
Ignore it. As long as I can still find my stuff, it's fine.
OCD suddenly kicks in. I must tidy it spotless right now.
There's order in the chaos. It's my unique personal style.

questions.text: How long has it been since you laughed with real joy?

options.text: Every day. My laugh threshold is super low.
A few days ago, over a funny meme.
Can't remember. Lately my laughs are all polite fake ones.
I've forgotten how to laugh from the heart.

questions.text: For you, what's the most effective way to 'recharge'?

options.text: Get out in nature, hike, see the sea, breathe fresh air.
Get a spa, massage, or hot spring, fully relax the body.
Immerse in my hobbies (gaming, drawing, stanning idols).
As long as I'm not working, anything recharges me.

results.title: Stress-Immune Constitution
results.description: Congrats, right now your body and mind are super healthy! You have excellent stress tolerance and emotional regulation. Even when you hit setbacks, you digest them fast and turn them into fuel. Your life is full of balance and fun.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Keep up this optimistic, easygoing mindset. You're the sun to your friends around you!

results.title: Mildly Tense State
results.description: You may have hit a few challenges lately, leaving your nerves a little on edge. But don't worry, it's still within what you can handle. Your body is reminding you it's time to slow down a bit.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Plan a little weekend getaway or go eat something delicious to clear out the fatigue you've been stacking up!

results.title: Pressure-Cooker Alarm
results.description: Watch out! Your stress index is already high and your body may be starting to show little issues. You tend to bottle things up, not wanting to burden others, but that only turns your emotions into a pressure cooker about to boil over.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Stop 'toughing it out' right now! Confide in someone you trust, or get a massage to loosen up. You badly need a release.

results.title: On the Brink of Breakdown
results.description: Danger! Your body and mind have hit their limit. You may be extremely anxious, sleepless, or world-weary. The tiniest outside spark could be the last straw that breaks the camel's back. You've forgotten how to relax.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Drop everything! Please seek professional counseling or take a long break. Stop forcing yourself to be strong.

title: The Other You in a Parallel Universe
description: In another timeline, what life would you be living? Reveal the dream you crave deep down but don't dare to chase.

questions.text: If you could go back and change one decision, what would you change?

options.text: Change my old decision about school or my major.
Bravely confess to my crush, or win back a lost relationship.
Tell past me: 'Go all in on that stock/Bitcoin, now!'
Change nothing. My experiences made me who I am.

questions.text: What job do you think the parallel-universe you is doing right now?

options.text: A superstar in the spotlight or an artist on stage.
A wandering backpacker or a freelance writer.
A lab-coat scientist or a world-changing entrepreneur.
An ordinary but happy office worker, just like now.

questions.text: In another timeline, how do you think your personality would differ from now?

options.text: Turned super outgoing and confident, the center of attention everywhere.
Turned cold and ruthless, living purely for myself.
Turned incredibly gentle and tender, caring for everyone like an angel.
Probably about the same. A leopard can't change its spots.

questions.text: If you could take one thing from now into the parallel universe, you'd bring:

options.text: All my current memories and experience points.
The people I love most (partner, family, or pet).
The money in my bank account (if it converts).
