A blank heart, to start fresh and experience it all anew.

questions.text: What do you think the love life of parallel-universe you is like?

options.text: Surrounded by countless admirers, an absolute heartthrob.
Living a fairytale life with a true soulmate.
Staying single to the end, savoring ultimate freedom.
Been through countless players, still searching for true love.

questions.text: If you got to meet the parallel-universe you, what's your first line?

options.text: 'Are you doing okay? Happier than me?'
'Quick, tell me your world's lottery numbers!'
'How did you end up like this? / How'd you get so gorgeous?'
Say nothing, just give them a deep, tight hug.

questions.text: Do you think parallel-universe you envies your current life?

options.text: Definitely! I'm not perfect, but I have amazing family and friends.
Probably not. Their life is likely ten thousand times more exciting.
We each have our own worries. We envy each other.
They wouldn't care at all. They're living just fine in their own world.

questions.text: On a planet with no gravity, what would you want to try?

options.text: Feel the thrill of soaring and flipping freely through the air.
Build a floating castle in mid-air.
Study how to eat spicy hot pot in zero gravity.
Way too unsettling. Find a way back to Earth, fast.

questions.text: If parallel-you comes begging for help, saying their world is about to be destroyed, you:

options.text: No hesitation! Cross the timelines with them to save the world.
Let them stay in my world. We'll live together.
Sympathetic, but it's too dangerous. Politely decline.
Suspect they're a scammer. Call the cops first, ask questions later.

questions.text: Do you believe there's another person in this world exactly like you?

options.text: Absolutely! The universe is huge, there must be another me.
That only happens in sci-fi movies.
Maybe, but our paths will never cross.
I'm one of a kind! No one could possibly be exactly like me.

results.title: The Content Guardian
results.description: You're very satisfied with the status quo. Life may not be lavishly rich, but you deeply cherish the people and things around you. You have few regrets and don't crave a thrilling, adventurous life. The parallel you would still be a gentle, kind, ordinary person.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Knowing how to be grateful and treasure the present is your greatest weapon for finding calm and happiness.

results.title: The Down-to-Earth Dreamer
results.description: You carry a few small regrets and unfinished dreams, but you won't flee reality over them. You believe the way to change your fate isn't crossing timelines, but the effort you put in now. You've found the perfect balance between the two universes.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: You have regrets, but you're always on the road to becoming better. Trust yourself. You're creating the best timeline.

results.title: The Freedom-Craving Wanderer
results.description: Deep down you long to break free of society's constraints and experience a different life. You feel a trace of dissatisfaction and weariness with the status quo, often fantasizing whether, in another parallel timeline, you'd live more freely and boldly.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: If you're unhappy with the status quo, start making small changes now! You don't need a parallel universe to be reborn.

results.title: The World-Shaking Visionary
results.description: Your imagination runs wild, bursting with disruptive, creative ideas! You strongly crave to change certain past decisions and firmly believe the parallel you is absolutely a world-shaking big shot. Your soul hungers for the ultimate experience.
results.comment: Exclusive Analysis: Your imagination is a priceless treasure. Try turning that wild vision into real creative work or a business. You'll amaze everyone!

title: "Work Smell" Concentration Test: Has Your Job Pickled You Yet?
description: "Work smell" is the aura that won't wash off — dead-tired eyes, a noise-cancelled soul, zero enthusiasm for anything. 10 questions to measure how deep the workplace brine has soaked in, and how much "after-hours life" you have left.

questions.text: The moment your alarm goes off in the morning, your very first thought is?

options.text: "A new day! Let's go make it count"
"Ugh, five more minutes. Just five"
"What day is it? How many days till Friday?"
Running the numbers: what would calling in sick cost me right now

questions.text: The current state of your closet?

options.text: A different look every day — getting dressed is how I recharge
A few go-to fits I love; I'll dress up when the mood strikes
Same rotation of "work uniforms" — convenience wins
Black, white, gray on infinite loop. Fashion is dead, long live comfy

questions.text: A friend invites you to dinner after work. Your honest reaction?

options.text: "I'm in!" Instant yes — I've been dying to talk to someone
Depends who's asking — I'll show up for people I like
"So tired today, rain check" (the rain check never comes)
Left on read. My soul is already on the train home

questions.text: What does Sunday night look like for you?

options.text: Excited for tomorrow — already planned what to crush this week
Totally chill. Monday is just another day
Getting antsy, revenge-scrolling until I can't bear to fall asleep
Full "Monday dread" mode — my chest tightens just thinking about clocking in

questions.text: When was the last time you genuinely laughed out loud (not a polite smile)?

options.text: Today, actually. Joy is everywhere
This week — my friend's memes still hit
Can't really remember... last month, maybe?
My smile muscles have atrophied; only the fake boss-smile is still in business

questions.text: Your default face in the office kitchen or elevator?

options.text: Smiling — I can chat with anyone I run into
Relaxed and natural; I say hi when hi is due
Completely blank, eyes glazed over in power-save mode
A do-not-approach storm cloud — coworkers auto-reroute around me

questions.text: "So what do you do after work?" Your answer is?

options.text: Gym, classes, hangouts — schedule packed to the brim
Sometimes shows, sometimes going out — whatever the mood says
Lie down, scroll my phone, look up and it's somehow bedtime
After work? Not sure my shift ever actually ends (keeps replying to messages)

questions.text: Someone mentions the word "dreams." Your internal monologue?

options.text: Eyes light up — I'm actively working toward mine
I still remember what it looks like; I take it out and dust it off sometimes
What's that? How much salary does it convert to?
Don't even. My only remaining dream is "never working again"

questions.text: You hear "quick meeting in a bit." Your physiological response?

options.text: Sure thing — we might actually hash out something good
Calm acceptance. It's part of the job
Deep breath in. Eyes go dead for a solid three seconds
Soul leaves body on the spot; the husk nods and says "sounds good"

questions.text: Surprise day off tomorrow — how do you spend it?

options.text: Bucket list locked and loaded, heading out immediately
A nice meal with friends — a full, satisfying day
Sleep till I wake up naturally, then rot away the rest
Catch up on sleep, shows, and my wrecked schedule — and somehow end the day more tired

results.title: [Scent-Free] Fresh-Picked Baby Greens
results.description: There's barely a whiff of work smell on you — work is just one part of your life, not the whole thing. You've still got real curiosity and fire for the world, and your after-hours life is rich enough to make coworkers jealous: hobbies, friends, things to look forward to. You know exactly why you work, and exactly who you are outside of it. This "more human than employee" state is a luxury most office veterans can never get back.
results.comment: Guard that freshness with your life! Treat your vacation plans and little life rituals as top-priority assets — they're your natural preservatives against getting pickled by the job.

results.title: [Lightly Seasoned] Gently Marinated Side Dish
results.description: You've started giving off a faint work smell: the occasional sigh, the occasional thousand-yard stare, dead eyes at the word "meeting." The good news: your life hasn't been fully annexed by your job. You still stay up late for things you love and still remember the shape of your dreams — you just take them out less often. You're standing right on the ridge between "office worker" and "person with a life." Which way you slide depends on where you spend your energy next.
results.comment: Prescription: force one "completely work-free thing to look forward to" into every week — a great meal, a movie night. Work smell washes out easiest while it's still faint.

results.title: [Fully Marinated] Old Braise, Eight-Tenths Done
results.description: The work smell has pickled you to the bone: grayscale uniform, leaving everyone on read, going horizontal the second you're home, smile only activated by payroll. Work seeps into every day like braising liquid — even your weekends are soaked in "compensatory rotting" that somehow leaves you more tired. The real warning sign: you've started treating exhaustion as your default setting, forgetting you weren't always like this.
results.comment: Time to launch Operation De-Marinate. Start small: pick one day a week to leave on time and kill your notifications, and let life's flavor cover the work smell again. You don't need one long vacation — you need a little slice of every day that's 100% yours.

results.title: [Vintage Grade] Ten-Year Braise Left on the Stove
results.description: Alert! Your work smell is off the charts — you've hit "everyone in the elevator can tell you've been doing overtime" levels. Your soul is in permanent standby: numb to joy, numb to vacations, too tired to even complain. This isn't just work smell anymore — it's the opening act of burnout, and your body is sending SOS signals through fatigue, numbness, and that storm cloud over your head. Seriously: put "yourself" back on the calendar. You are a person, not headcount.
results.comment: Strong recommendation: take an actual vacation (not the lying-down-scrolling kind). And if the exhaustion has been wrecking your sleep and mood for more than two weeks, talking to a professional is a genuinely good move. The work will never be finished — but there's only one of you.

title: Alive-Person Energy Check: Is Your Soul Still Open for Business?
description: "Alive-person energy" — light in your eyes, real reactions to the world, posts that sound like an actual human. It's the rarest vibe of the social media era. 10 questions to gauge your soul's freshness: are you certified fresh-catch alive, or a cardboard cutout that leaves everyone on read?

questions.text: You spot a flower blooming ridiculously well on the roadside. You...

options.text: Stop for a proper look — maybe even snap a pic for a friend
Internal "whoa," but keep walking
I think I saw it? Felt nothing
Flower? What flower? I was on my phone

questions.text: The recent state of your social media accounts?

options.text: Life fragments and random thoughts, posted on impulse — very warm-blooded
Occasional stories for moments worth remembering
Basically just reposting other people's stuff; I don't really speak
Last post dates back to the ancient era; the account has grown weeds

questions.text: Friends start on a topic you know nothing about (a new show, some fandom drama). You...

options.text: "Wait, what is that? Tell me everything!" Curiosity: fully online
Find myself getting interested, quietly look it up at home
Polite nodding, zero ripples inside
Auto-switch to mute mode and wait for the topic to pass

questions.text: When you eat something amazing, your reaction is...

options.text: Audible, on the spot: "This is INSANELY good!"
Eyes light up — immediately offer a bite to whoever's next to me
Think "not bad," then finish it in silence
The only difference between good and bad food is how fast I eat it

questions.text: Your last "just because" move (a detour to catch the sunset, a sudden urge to learn something new) was when?

options.text: This week — my life runs on spontaneous impulses
Last month, and it still makes me smile
Hmm, let me think... six months ago?
Why would I do something with no purpose?

questions.text: A friend sends you a funny video. You usually...

options.text: Laugh out loud and fire back three funnier ones
Genuinely crack up and reply with a wall of "HAHAHAHA"
Slight smirk, send one "haha" to fulfill my social duty
Seen. Thinking: what exactly is funny about this

questions.text: How do you feel about "tomorrow"?

options.text: There's something to look forward to — I smile just thinking about it before sleep
Ordinary, but I can always find some small thing to anticipate
Tomorrow is just copy-paste of today
I'd rather it not come. Just let me sleep forever

questions.text: You hear a song you love while walking down the street. You...

options.text: Steps sync to the beat, can't help humming along
Instant mood lift — quietly crank the volume
"Oh, this song," then back to spacing out
No idea what's playing in my earbuds. I wasn't listening

questions.text: Someone compliments you sincerely. Your reaction?

options.text: Can't hide the joy — big thank you and a compliment right back
Shy but grinning, secretly happy about it all day
"It's whatever." Zero ripples inside
Alert: what are they about to ask me for?

questions.text: Alone late at night, your most common state is...

options.text: Grateful for today, itching for tomorrow
Calm and cozy, enjoying my own little world
Brain auto-plays "Today's Most Awkward Moments: Greatest Hits"
No emotions. Literally. None

results.title: [Soul in Hibernation] NPC Standby Mode
results.description: Your alive-person reading is hovering near zero: numb to food, numb to compliments, numb to tomorrow — like someone set you to mute. But take note: this usually isn't "just how you are." Long-running exhaustion, stress, or disappointment has flipped your feelings into protective shutdown. Your soul isn't gone — it's hibernating, waiting for a safe moment to come back online.
results.comment: No rush on the reboot — start micro: actually taste one meal today; before bed, write down one thing that was "okay." If the numbness sticks around and starts disrupting your life, talking to someone you trust or a mental health professional is a brave move, not a weak one.

results.title: [Power-Save Mode] Half-Thawed Fresh Fish
results.description: Your alive-person energy flickers on and off, like a phone in low-power mode: a real smile occasionally escapes around close friends, but most of the time a single "haha" carries the whole conversation. It's not that you feel nothing — you've just trained yourself to compress every feeling to minimum volume, because expressing is exhausting, hoping risks disappointment, and caring risks getting hurt. But deep down you know: those "can't be bothered to say it" moments are hiding a whole lot of unspoken aliveness.
results.comment: Try one "over-reaction" a day: if it's delicious, say so out loud; if it's funny, actually laugh. Expression is a muscle — build it back, and you'll notice the world warming up to you in return.

results.title: [Fresh Catch Grade] Certified Alive, Eyes Full of Light
results.description: Congrats — you're the rarest resource of the social media era: a genuine, certified alive person! You make noises over good food, get a mood lift from a single song, and laugh for real at your friends' terrible jokes. Your emotions flow in and out, back and forth, and everyone around you can feel it: this person is really listening, really laughing. That authentic responsiveness is the beating heart of alive-person energy.
results.comment: Your freshness is beautifully preserved. One note: alive people need rest too. Give yourself permission to be a quiet NPC for a day now and then — you don't have to be open for business 24/7. That's how the aliveness lasts.

results.title: [Supernova Grade] Walking Dopamine
results.description: Your alive-person energy is overflowing! The light in your eyes could illuminate an entire street, and your curiosity runs permanently overclocked: a roadside flower, a new shop, a friend's random topic — everything ignites you. You don't just live at full boil yourself; you're contagious with it. You're the walking dopamine of your friend group, a human solar panel — even the air around you tastes sweeter.
results.comment: You're the rare of the rare — but remember: not everyone runs at your clock speed. Slow down a beat sometimes and wait for your power-save-mode friends. Your warmth hits even harder when it isn't rushing.

title: Adulting Level Check: Are You an Entry-Level Adult or an Advanced Adult?
description: The viral phrase of the year — "entry-level adults" are desperate to prove they've grown up, while "advanced adults" have already made peace with life. 10 real-life scenarios to reveal which tier your adulting has reached.

questions.text: The restaurant brings you the wrong dish. You...

options.text: Too embarrassed to say anything — quietly eat the wrong order
Demand a replacement at maximum intensity to establish "don't mess with me"
Ask them to fix it, then feel awkward for the rest of the meal
Smile, let the server know, get it swapped — and let it go

questions.text: It's payday. Your very first move?

options.text: Treat myself first! Check out everything that's been sitting in the cart
Pay the bills; whatever's left flows wherever it flows
Budget and track everything diligently... and still overspend by month's end
Auto-transfer a fixed cut to savings and investments, then spend the rest guilt-free

questions.text: A friend cancels on you last minute. Your reaction?

options.text: "No worries!" out loud, permanent entry in my mental grudge ledger
Sulk and leave them on read for the next few days
Bummed, but immediately draft an excellent solo plan B
Tell them straight — "hey, that didn't feel great" — then talk it out

questions.text: Facing a relationship (friendship or romance) that no longer fits you, you...

options.text: Keep it going — we go way back, and cutting it off feels like a waste
Slow fade, letting the read receipts deliver the message
Lay it all out even if things get ugly — it needs a proper ending
Part on good terms, let go with gratitude, and save the seat for the right person

questions.text: When you're feeling emo in the middle of the night, you...

options.text: Dump my feelings into a story post, then panic-delete it in the morning
White-knuckle it — "thinking about this is useless" — and shove the feelings down
Trauma-dump to someone and somehow feel even messier afterward
Let myself be sad for a bit, write it down or sleep on it, and deal with it tomorrow

questions.text: Seeing peers doing better than you (buying homes, getting promoted, traveling the world), your honest feeling is...

options.text: Anxiety-induced insomnia — I start questioning my entire life
Congratulations on the surface, a little salty in private
A twinge of envy, then quickly back to my own pace
Genuinely happy for them — because I know exactly what I want

questions.text: When you're sick and feeling awful, you...

options.text: Power through work or class — taking leave feels like a hassle, and I don't want to look like I can't handle pressure
Wait until it gets really bad, then grudgingly see a doctor
See the doctor and take the meds, but lie there with my head still full of work
Take the day off, see the doctor, sleep properly — my body is my most important asset

questions.text: How do you feel about admitting fault and apologizing?

options.text: Apologizing is humiliating — find an excuse and hold the line first
I'll apologize, but always with a "but you were wrong too" attached
I'm willing to apologize, but then I spiral about whether I'm a failure
If I'm wrong, I own it sincerely and do better — that's not shameful, it builds credit

questions.text: Facing relatives' "concern" (When are you getting married? How much do you make?), you...

options.text: Awkward polite laughter, followed by three days of internal damage
Visible attitude or a clapback on the spot — the room instantly freezes
Deploy a well-rehearsed script and deflect until they give up
Smile and set the boundary — "I'll keep that one to myself~" — then steer the conversation elsewhere

questions.text: Your deepest takeaway about growing up?

options.text: Growing up is nonstop losing, and I'm not ready for it
Growing up means holding it together — never let the exhaustion show
Growing up is learning to make trade-offs, even when letting go hurts
Growing up is finally being able to take care of myself — with warmth left over for others

results.title: [Adult-in-Training] A Kid Holding a Grown-Up ID
results.description: Your body is legally an adult, but the kid inside is still clutching your sleeve. You dread conflict, can't speak up when you're shortchanged, and your first instinct against unfairness is to endure it — the adult world just feels impossibly hard. Here's the truth: it's not that you're not good enough. Nobody ever actually taught us how to be adults. Right now you're more of an "adult by obligation" — the uniform is on, but the operating manual never arrived.
results.comment: Step one of practice: voice your needs, even quietly. Wrong dish? Ask for the swap. Feeling sick? Take the day off. You'll find the world doesn't hate you for standing up for yourself — it starts respecting you.

results.title: [Entry-Level Adult] The Adult Trying Very Hard to Prove It
results.description: You're a textbook "entry-level adult": working overtime to look mature, tough, and bulletproof — as if one crack of softness means losing. You defend your rights at maximum force, compress emotions into internal injuries, and spiral when others are thriving — because somewhere along the way, you decided adulthood is a competition you must win. But real maturity isn't armor; it's ease. You already have an adult's capabilities. What you need to train next is an adult's breathing room.
results.comment: A line just for you: "You don't have to keep proving you're an adult. You already are one." Try redirecting some of that armor-building energy into rest and self-acceptance.

results.title: [Mid-Level Adult] A Life Player Hitting Their Stride
results.description: You've outgrown the "trying way too hard at adulthood" phase and started finding your own rhythm: you know how to say no, how to manage your money, and how to cut yourself slack on emo nights. Sure, you still overthink sometimes, still envy people, still question your life at 2 a.m. — but your recovery speed keeps getting faster. You're on the road from "holding it together" to "genuinely at ease," and the advanced-adult ticket is already waving at you.
results.comment: The one class you still need: "permission to be imperfect." The gap between mid-level and advanced usually isn't ability — it's whether you can gently let yourself off the hook.

results.title: [Advanced Adult] The At-Ease Player Who Made Peace with Life
results.description: Congrats — you've reached the legendary "advanced adult" tier: wrong dish, no rage; boundary crossed, calmly redrawn; others succeed, genuine cheers; sadness shows up, and you let it stay a while. Your maturity isn't armor held up by willpower — it's ease from the inside out, because you know exactly who you are, what you want, and what you don't. Rarest of all: after taking good care of yourself, you still have warmth left over to catch other people when they fall.
results.comment: You're already what a lot of people are trying to become. One reminder: advanced adults are allowed to fall apart too. Occasionally dropping the composure and being a bratty kid for one night — that's also part of being advanced.

title: Delulu Index: Is Your Inner Theater Out of Control?
description: "Delulu is the solulu" — the confidence gospel that took over the internet. The right dose of delusion is courage fuel; an overdose is a social emergency. 10 questions to measure exactly how much fan-fiction your brain is writing.

questions.text: Your crush read your message 3 hours ago and hasn't replied. Your interpretation?

options.text: They're busy, or just don't feel like replying. Both totally normal
Slightly bummed, but I go do my own thing
Re-reading our entire chat history line by line, hunting for evidence they secretly like me
They obviously like me SO much they're too nervous to reply!

questions.text: The interview ends with "we'll be in touch." You think...

options.text: Standard closing line. No assumptions
Replay my performance and do a little post-mortem
They smiled at me twice — this is basically in the bag!
Already planning my first-day outfit and how to spend the year-end bonus

questions.text: Your mental state when buying lottery or scratch-off tickets?

options.text: Pure entertainment — forgotten the moment I buy it
A tiny daydream before the draw, a shrug and a laugh after
Already drafted the opening line of my post-jackpot resignation letter
This round feels DIFFERENT! I'm already browsing real estate

questions.text: Your favorite celebrity glances toward your section at a concert. You...

options.text: They were looking at the big screen. Nothing to do with me
Little thrill — this ticket just paid for itself!
"That was ABSOLUTELY eye contact" — and I tell my friends about it for three straight days
A bond has formed between us. They will remember me forever

questions.text: Before starting a new project (losing weight, learning a language, saving money), you...

options.text: Set realistic small goals and proceed step by step
Set a slightly ambitious target to motivate myself
Fantasize about my total glow-up in three months — and post the announcement first
Throw the victory party in my head, get moved to tears — then start tomorrow

questions.text: A friend says "you're glowing today." Your internal translation?

options.text: Polite small talk. Say thanks
Yes! The skincare routine is finally working
Have I gotten hotter lately? No wonder strangers keep looking at me twice
I might be an undiscovered natural beauty. Is it too late to debut?

questions.text: Have you ever rehearsed an award acceptance speech or a "post-fame interview" in your head?

options.text: Never. Why would I practice that?
The thought flashes by occasionally, then I laugh at myself
I keep a few standard versions — shower time is rehearsal time
Lines, blocking, the exact timing of my voice cracking — all choreographed and stage-ready

questions.text: You see a post like "Mercury retrograde is over — your luck is on the way." You...

options.text: Scroll past. Not really a believer
Give it a look, just for peace of mind
Screenshot it and repost to my story: "Claiming this energy!"
Total belief. Standing by for destiny — even the numbers from my dream go straight onto a lottery ticket

questions.text: Someone shoots down your idea. Your first reaction?

options.text: Hear them out — if it makes sense, I'll take it on board
A little defensive, but I'll go chew on it later
"They just can't see my vision yet"
Genius is always lonely. History will prove me right

questions.text: Your stance on "dreams coming true"?

options.text: Dreams take planning and execution, not wishful thinking
I believe in hard work, plus a little bit of luck
Law of attraction! Believe first, and the universe handles the logistics
My life is a binge-worthy drama — the main-character arc will activate any day now

results.title: [Painfully Sober] Documentary Director Personality
results.description: Your brain is a high-resolution documentary camera: left on read means they didn't want to reply, interview scripts are just scripts, and the lottery is an entertainment tax. You don't embellish, don't apply filters, don't move yourself to tears — you live strictly in the land of verified evidence. It keeps you rarely hurt and razor-sharp in judgment: the designated "sober friend" of the group. But occasionally, all that clarity costs you a little "irrational courage" — some opportunities only become visible after you choose to believe in them.
results.comment: Your prescription: allow yourself one delulu moment per month. Before any big decision, try asking, "If success were guaranteed, what would I do?" A measured dose of delusion is the clear-eyed person's cheat code.

results.title: [Pleasantly Buzzed] The Healthy Moderate Dreamer
results.description: You've nailed delulu's golden ratio: you fantasize, but never fully commit to the bit; you hope, but always keep an exit. A little post-interview optimism here, a five-minute resignation-letter daydream after buying a lottery ticket there — these measured doses of brain sugar keep life sweet without unmooring you from reality. Psychology calls this "positive illusion," and in moderation it's genuinely good for you. You're the social drinker of the delusion world: pleasantly buzzed, never blacked out.
results.comment: Keep this exact formula! One available upgrade: convert your daydreams into concrete action lists, and your "inner theater" levels up into a trailer for real life.

results.title: [Resident Screenwriter] Inner Theater Flagship Store
results.description: Your brain runs prime-time drama 24/7: one glance becomes a three-episode arc, one "you're glowing" becomes proof you should debut as an idol. A solid sixty percent of your daily happiness comes from the theater in your head, and your award speech and interview lines have been rehearsal-ready for years. Good news: that lush imagination makes you self-charging and remarkably hard to demoralize. Bad news: with the script this packed, you occasionally forget the real-life plot needs you to physically move it forward.
results.comment: The script is fantastic — now roll cameras! Rule of thumb: every finished mental blockbuster earns one matching real-world micro-action. Send that message; sign up for that class. Delusion that lands in reality gets a new name: vision.

results.title: [Final Form] The Chosen One of Delulu
results.description: Congratulations (?) — you have ascended to peak delulu: being left on read is evidence of a secret crush, an idol's stray glance is destiny, and your main-character aura idles on permanent standby. You live inside a self-written, self-directed, self-starring hit drama; your happiness levels are enviable and your confidence is unbreakable — rejected a hundred times, you'd simply conclude the universe is filtering out the unworthy. That thick skin and optimism are genuinely rare gifts. Just be careful: when the gap between the script and reality gets wide enough to need damage control, the fall can hurt more than usual.
results.comment: Keep the main-character aura, but hire a "reality-check consigliere" — one friend who dares to tell you the truth. Before any big money, love, or career decision, let them finish talking before you start filming. Delulu is the solulu — but the solulu needs a pinch of fact-checking for seasoning.

title: Dating Flag Check: Are You a Red Flag, Green Flag, or Beige Flag?
description: Red flag, green flag, beige flag — the internet's favorite way to size up a partner. 10 brutally honest questions: in your partner's eyes, are you a warning sign to run from, or a keeper worth holding onto?

questions.text: They read your message and only replied three hours later. Your move?

options.text: Interrogation — "why didn't you reply?" — plus a barrage of back-to-back calls
Leave THEM hanging for exactly three hours. We're even
It bugs me a little, but I'll bring it up casually next time we meet
Everyone has their own rhythm — I don't read into it

questions.text: Mid-argument, your most common move is...

options.text: Dredge up old scores plus personal jabs — win first, talk later
Go straight to silent treatment and vanish, waiting to be coaxed back
Hold it all in and quietly bruise on the inside
Stick to the issue: "I feel..." instead of "You always..."

questions.text: When their ex comes up, your attitude is...

options.text: Full background check plus periodic surprise phone inspections
"Don't care" on the surface — privately excavated the ex's IG back three years
Curious, quietly comparing myself, never saying it out loud
The past is the past. What matters is they're with me now

questions.text: You're running late for a date. You...

options.text: I'll get there when I get there. A little waiting won't kill them
Blame it on traffic — definitely not my fault
Apologize sincerely... though next time I'll probably be late again
Text ahead with an apology and live ETA updates, then make it up in person

questions.text: They're going to dinner with a friend of the opposite sex. Your reaction?

options.text: Hard no. That, or we break up
They can go, but with location sharing on plus check-ins every half hour
Say "have fun," then spend the entire night restlessly refreshing my phone
Trust them completely and genuinely hope they have a great time

questions.text: Your style with money in a relationship?

options.text: Their money is our money; my money is still my money
Whoever loves more pays — paying is proof of love
I'll split things, but I keep a silent ledger of who's paid more
Agree on a split that feels good to both of us — generous and unbothered

questions.text: You realize you wrongly accused them. You...

options.text: Never admit it — change the subject and flip the blame while I'm at it
Brush it off with a quick "fine, fine, whatever"
Too embarrassed to say it outright, so I quietly compensate by being extra nice
Apologize properly and spell out exactly where my thinking went wrong

questions.text: How do you run this relationship on social media?

options.text: Hide my relationship status — keeps my "options open"
Audit every single like they hand out, one by one
Post couple content, but mostly to mark my territory
Share naturally, and respect how visible they want to be

questions.text: They're slammed at work lately and have less time for you. You...

options.text: Threaten a breakup to test whether they really love me
Start itemized scorekeeping — "you never spend time with me" — and replay that ledger daily
Understanding on the outside, quietly hurt on the inside, until one day it all erupts
Say what I need, problem-solve together — and keep my own life running well meanwhile

questions.text: Your attitude toward commitment (meeting the parents, planning a future)?

options.text: Commitment is a tool for locking someone down — secure first, ask questions later
Stall as long as possible; deflect whenever it comes up
I have thoughts, but I'm scared saying them out loud will scare them off
When the timing feels right, talk about it honestly — let them see the roadmap

results.title: [Red Flag Flagship Store] A Walking Red Alert
results.description: Diagnosis: red lights flashing. Control, score-dredging, emotional blackmail, allergic to apologizing — the high-risk behaviors of dating, and you may have gotten disturbingly fluent in them. But before you rip up this result: behind most red-flag behavior stands someone who is terrified of being left. Control comes from insecurity; tests come from not daring to ask for love directly. You're not a bad person — you're just holding on with a grip that pushes people away. Flags can be swapped. Step one is looking at yours honestly.
results.comment: Start by trading interrogation for expression: swap "why didn't you reply?" for "when you go quiet that long, I get a little worried." And if you notice the same spiral repeating in every relationship, talking to a counselor isn't admitting defeat — it's leveling up.

results.title: [Orange Alert Zone] The Half-Cooked Lover Who Occasionally Smokes
results.description: You're not a red flag, but you are wearing a few orange ones: silent treatments, undercover jealousy, psychological warfare via read receipts. You actually know what healthy love looks like — but when the feelings surge, you still swap "saying it" for "making them guess." Your love is real; it just insists on taking the scenic route. You're waiting for them to get it, they're waiting for you to say it, and in the end you're both exhausted.
results.comment: You have exactly one homework assignment: upgrade "guess" to "say." Next time the cold-war urge hits, send this first: "I'm feeling off and need a little space — but this is not a cold war." That single step is the difference between half-cooked and fully done.

results.title: [Certified Beige Flag] The Room-Temperature-Water Partner, Maximum Security
results.description: Congrats — there's barely a red flag on you. In their place: a full row of beige flags. Stable, reasonable, boundary-respecting; on time for dates, calm in arguments, transparent with money. You're the blue-chip stock of the dating market. The only catch: stability sometimes gets misread as blandness, and thoughtfulness gets taken for granted. What you've never lacked is character — you're just missing a small dash of "didn't see that coming" spark.
results.comment: Your fundamentals are unbeatable, so add a curveball: every once in a while, plan a surprise for absolutely no reason and let them see your unhinged-adorable side. Beige plus one pop of color is the combination that never gets old.

results.title: [Green Flag Dream] The Textbook Ideal Partner
results.description: All green, full marks! You've collected the complete healthy-relationship set: emotionally steady, communicates directly, respects freedom, apologizes when wrong, transparent about commitment. Dating you feels like breathing mountain air — safe, clean, and somehow still romantic. It's not that you have no temper; you've learned to translate temper into words. It's not that you never get jealous; you choose to trust first and verify gently. Partners like you are an endangered species — your other half had better appreciate it.
results.comment: One reminder: the classic green-flag pitfall is shouldering all the understanding by yourself. You're allowed to feel hurt too — a healthy relationship means both people flying green flags, not you holding the flag up alone.

title: The Professional MBTI 16 Personality Types Test
description: 20 scenario-based questions spanning the four core dimensions—where you get your energy (E/I), how you take in information (S/N), how you make decisions (T/F), and how you approach life (J/P)—weighted to reveal which of the 16 personality types you are. Every type comes with a deep dive into your cognitive wiring, strengths and blind spots, love and relationships, career fit, and personalized growth tips.

questions.text: After a long, busy week, how do you usually recharge?

options.text: Round up a group of friends for dinner, karaoke, or a big hangout—the livelier, the more energized I feel
Grab coffee with one or two close friends and catch up—just right
Depends on my mood, but more often I'd rather have some quiet time alone
Phone off, alone, doing nothing—that's what actually refills my tank

questions.text: At a party where you barely know anyone, you'll most likely?

options.text: Jump right in and work the room, striking up conversations with different people
Observe for a bit first, then find a chance to join a group I click with
Stick close to the people I already know and chat once I'm introduced
Find a corner to zone out and quietly count down to when I can leave

questions.text: When you're working through a problem that takes some thought, your habit is closer to?

options.text: Thinking out loud—pulling people in to talk it through helps me get clearer
Bouncing a few ideas off someone, then circling back to sort it out
Working out the gist on my own first, then checking with someone
Having to think it all the way through in private before I'll say a word

questions.text: Which of these best describes your everyday self?

options.text: Talkative and expressive—people can read my mood at a glance
Actually pretty chatty once I've warmed up to you
Not much of a talker—I'm usually the one listening
Quiet and reserved—a lot going on inside, but I rarely show it

questions.text: After several days on your own, how do you feel?

options.text: About to lose it—I'm dying for some human interaction
Starting to miss the buzz, so I'll reach out and make plans
Pretty content, though I'll want to talk to someone now and then
Loving it—honestly, I hope it lasts a little longer

questions.text: When someone's telling you about something, what do you notice first?

options.text: The concrete details—who, what, when, where, the facts
I lock onto the facts first, then think a bit about what they mean
As I listen, my mind drifts to the meaning or possibilities behind it
I jump straight to 'what does this signify and where is it headed'

questions.text: Faced with a brand-new idea, your first reaction leans toward?

options.text: Asking 'how would this actually work, and is it even doable?'
Finding it interesting, but quickly pulling it back to a reality check
Excitedly running with all the possibilities it opens up
Dreaming up everything it could become—details can come later

questions.text: Which kind of information do you trust more?

options.text: Concrete, verified experience I've seen with my own eyes
Mostly the facts, but I factor in gut instinct too
Mostly my instincts, though I'll look for some facts to back them up
The flashes of insight and overall vibe in my head—they're often uncannily right

questions.text: When learning something new, what approach suits you best?

options.text: Give me clear steps and real examples, and I'll follow along
Steps first, ideally with a bit of the reasoning behind them
Let me grasp the underlying concept, and I'll fill in the details myself
Get me the big-picture framework and principles first—I hate rote-memorizing steps

questions.text: When you look back on a past experience, what usually comes to mind?

options.text: Vivid images and details—what was said, what we ate
I remember the main facts, though the details get a little fuzzy
I remember the overall mood of the moment more than the specifics
The details are gone—I just remember the feeling and what it meant

questions.text: When making an important decision, what do you rely on first?

options.text: Logic, data, and an objective weighing of pros and cons
Mostly reason, but I fold in a bit of how I feel
Mostly how I feel, but I factor in the practical realities
How it affects people, and where it sits with my inner values

questions.text: When a friend vents to you, your instinct is to first?

options.text: Help them break down the problem and offer solutions
Offer advice first, then throw in some reassurance
Comfort them first, then work out a plan together
Wholeheartedly feel with them and let them know I get it

questions.text: When you have to give someone negative feedback, you tend to?

options.text: Lay out the problem and the facts directly—it's about the issue, not the person
Be honest above all, but pick my words carefully
Soften and package it to spare their feelings as much as possible
Care so much about their feelings that I'd rather be vague than hurt them

questions.text: What matters most in a good team decision, in your view?

options.text: Fair, consistent principles and the most efficient solution
Sound reasoning first, and it has to hold up
Accounting for how members feel, so no one's left uncomfortable
Preserving team harmony and everyone's sense of buy-in matters most

questions.text: What are people more likely to get wrong about you?

options.text: That I'm too level-headed, sometimes coming off as cold
Occasionally being told I'm too blunt
Occasionally being told I overthink and care too much about others
That I'm too driven by emotion and easily swept away by my feelings

questions.text: On a trip, which style do you enjoy more?

options.text: Itinerary, lodging, and transport all locked in—following the plan feels safest
Nail down the general direction and must-see spots, figure out the rest on the fly
Book the flight and go—everything else, I take as it comes
No plan at all—wandering wherever the moment takes me is the thrill

questions.text: Faced with a task that has a deadline, you'll most likely?

options.text: Plan early and finish in stages—never leaving it to the last minute
Set a schedule and usually wrap up a little ahead of time
Take it easy early on and hit the gas as the deadline nears
Go into overdrive at the last second—the more pressure, the more efficient I get

questions.text: Your room or desk is usually?

options.text: Sorted and tidy, everything in its place—mess makes my skin crawl
Mostly neat, and I tidy up on a regular basis
Looks a bit messy, but I know exactly where everything is
Organized chaos (okay, just chaos)—but it doesn't bother me one bit

questions.text: When plans get thrown off at the last minute, your reaction is?

options.text: Really irritated—I need to reshuffle fast and get back on track
A little thrown, but I'll adjust as quickly as I can
No big deal—rolling with the changes comes easily to me
Perfect—last-minute curveballs actually fire me up

questions.text: When facing a choice you haven't figured out yet, you lean toward?

options.text: Locking it down ASAP—leaving things unresolved makes me anxious
Deciding sooner rather than later, but giving myself a little time
Preferring to keep some flexibility before committing
Keeping things open-ended—staying flexible leaves room for more possibilities

results.title: INTJ · Architect
results.tagline: A strategist with both imagination and follow-through, always keeping a meticulous blueprint ready in their head.
results.rarity: About 2% of the population—one of the rarest types, and INTJ women are rarer still.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Introverted Intuition (Ni), lets you cut through the surface to grasp the long-term nature and trajectory of things; your auxiliary function, Extraverted Thinking (Te), breaks abstract insights down into efficient, executable systems. Your tertiary function (Fi) gives you a firm internal code, while your inferior function (Se) makes you less comfortable living in the moment and handling on-the-spot sensory details.
results.description: You're a born strategist. Your mind is like a chessboard perpetually running the next moves—while everyone else is fixed on the present, you've already foreseen how it plays out three steps ahead. You're independent, rational, and hold yourself and the world to sky-high standards, chasing efficiency and airtight logic in everything. You don't need applause—just a problem worth cracking. Rather than drifting with the current, you'd rather spend the time building a complete system of your own, then quietly and resolutely make it real.
strengths: Powerful strategic and systems thinking—you see several moves ahead and position yourself early
Highly independent and self-disciplined—once you lock onto a goal, you tune out distractions and see it through
Rational and objective, not easily swayed by mood swings or peer pressure
Hungry to learn, always leveling up, never satisfied with your skills or knowledge
challenges: Can come across as cold and distant, with stiff emotional expression that keeps people at arm's length
Little patience for those who seem 'not smart or hardworking enough'—your standards run too high
Overconfident in your own views, sometimes tuning out dissenting voices
Often underestimate how much emotions and relationships actually weigh in decisions and connections
results.relationship: In love and friendship, you prize quality over quantity—few friends, but every one a true confidant. You're no good at sweet nothings, but you protect the people you love with long-term planning, dependable commitments, and quietly solving their problems. What you need is a partner who respects your independent space and can go deep in conversation. Learning to say the 'I care' out loud, instead of just thinking it, is your single most effective move for closing the distance.
results.career: You thrive in fields that call for long-range planning, independent reasoning, and deep expertise: strategy consulting, scientific research, systems/engineering architecture, investment analysis, executive decision-making, and entrepreneurship. You shine in autonomous, expertise-driven, goal-clear environments—and can't stand drawn-out pointless meetings, rigid red tape, or illogical management.
results.growth: Your rationality is a superpower—just don't let it become an island. Try treating 'expressing feelings' as another project to conquer, letting the people close to you see the caring beneath your calm exterior. Remember: not everything worthwhile can be optimized—some relationships just need you present, not you fixing them.

results.title: INTP · Logician
results.tagline: Endlessly asking the world 'why,' with a precision thought-laboratory running inside their head.
results.rarity: About 3% of the population—overrepresented among thinkers and scientists.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Introverted Thinking (Ti), builds rigorous, internally consistent logical models in your mind; your auxiliary function, Extraverted Intuition (Ne), constantly reaches outward to connect new possibilities and hypotheses. Your tertiary Si and inferior Fe leave you a bit clumsy with interpersonal emotions and everyday practicalities.
results.description: You're an explorer of ideas—in love with concepts, theories, and abstract problems, able to spot the tiniest contradiction in an argument at a glance. Truth matters more to you than saving face; you'd sooner admit you're wrong than accept a conclusion that doesn't hold up. Your world is built from countless 'what ifs,' and a single question can lead you into an entire forest of thought. You don't much care about worldly achievements or conventions—only whether something actually makes sense.
strengths: Deep logical and abstract analysis—skilled at dismantling complex problems
Independent, objective, and unswayed by authority—you challenge established frameworks
An endless stream of creativity and cross-disciplinary connections
Astonishing depth of focus and study on topics that fascinate you
challenges: Prone to getting stuck 'thinking' and stalling on 'doing'—short on follow-through
Find daily chores, time management, and interpersonal emotions tricky
Procrastinate in pursuit of the perfect understanding, or keep getting sidetracked by new problems
Often skip the context when communicating, leaving people unable to follow your train of thought
results.relationship: You're genuine and unpretentious in relationships, and you loathe emotional manipulation and phony socializing. Your love language is sharing your mental world and exploring interesting questions together. You need a partner who gives you room to think and won't be hurt by the occasional zoning out. Practicing proactively responding to your partner's emotional needs is the most worthwhile homework you can do.
results.career: You suit fields with intellectual freedom and depth: research and academia, programming, data science, philosophy, systems analysis, writing, and any work that demands deep reasoning. You need flexibility and autonomy—your worst nightmare is repetitive, rigidly regulated work heavy on interpersonal coordination.
results.growth: There's treasure in your head, but only ideas that land in the real world have value. Set yourself a 'good enough, ship it' threshold and don't let perfectionism eat your momentum. And don't forget: people aren't equations to be solved—sometimes dropping the analysis and simply being present rewards a relationship with unexpected warmth.

results.title: ENTJ · Commander
results.tagline: A born leader who can't resist stepping in to fix inefficiency and chaos, then charging ahead with everyone in tow.
results.rarity: About 3% of the population—markedly overrepresented in leadership and management.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Extraverted Thinking (Te), efficiently organizes resources, orchestrates the big picture, and chases results; your auxiliary function, Introverted Intuition (Ni), locks onto a long-range vision and strategic direction. Your tertiary Se and inferior Fi make you less attuned to the subtle emotions of yourself and others.
results.description: You're a born leader. Strong-willed and decisive, you can't resist stepping in when you see inefficiency and chaos. Challenges aren't stress to you—they're fuel; you believe almost no problem can't be solved with strategy plus willpower. You excel at breaking grand visions into clear milestones, then leading a group to push them forward efficiently. Standing at the front and directing the whole show isn't a burden for you—it's second nature.
strengths: Outstanding decisiveness and command—you shoulder responsibility at the crucial moment
The drive to turn vision into concrete plans and forcefully make them real
Confident and bold, with a natural presence that rallies and persuades
Efficient and pragmatic—skilled at integrating resources, clearing obstacles, and hitting goals
challenges: Can come off as domineering or impatient, unintentionally steamrolling others
In the rush for efficiency, you overlook team members' emotions and pace
Not patient enough to listen—you tend to dominate conversations and rush to conclusions
You weigh work achievement so heavily you may neglect your life and intimate relationships
results.relationship: In relationships you're loyal, reliable, and dependable, folding your partner's life into your long-term plans. But you tend to 'solve' rather than 'listen,' sometimes treating love itself as a project to manage. Learning to let go of control, to simply be present and show vulnerability, lets your other half truly enter your inner world—rather than standing outside the fortress you've built.
results.career: You're a born manager and entrepreneur, suited to running businesses, leading projects and teams, strategic planning, law, finance, and any high-pressure field that demands decisiveness and coordination. You're in your element where you can wield influence, with clear goals and clear consequences—and you can't tolerate passive, sluggish, directionless organizations.
results.growth: You're built to win, but a true leader gets others wanting to win alongside them. Slow down half a beat to listen, and to ask rather than command—your influence upgrades from 'being obeyed' to 'being trusted.' Remember: the occasional show of vulnerability isn't losing control—it's the door that lets people get close to you.

results.title: ENTP · Debater
results.tagline: A fireworks factory of ideas who loves playing devil's advocate—all to force out a better answer.
results.rarity: About 3% of the population—quite common among entrepreneurs and creatives.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Extraverted Intuition (Ne), instantly sees countless possibilities and connections; your auxiliary function, Introverted Thinking (Ti), takes on the job of dissecting, testing, and reassembling those ideas. Your tertiary Fe and inferior Si make you impatient with routine details and long-term consistency.
results.description: You're sharp-minded and silver-tongued, and you love being the one who plays devil's advocate in a conversation—not to pick fights, but to force out a better answer. Rules and conventions are just 'defaults that haven't been challenged yet' to you. Your brain is like an engine that never idles, always wiring together unrelated concepts into startlingly new ideas. You relish the thrill of brainstorming, treating every clash of ideas as a fun game.
strengths: An endless stream of creativity and rapid cross-disciplinary connections
Powerful debating, adaptability, and on-the-spot persuasion
Happy to challenge the status quo—always producing framework-breaking new solutions
Quick-witted, funny, and a natural at getting a room going
challenges: Prone to fizzling out—your interests shift even faster than your ideas, making follow-through hard
Debating for the sake of debating, unintentionally stepping on others' emotional landmines
You hate repetition and detail—execution and discipline are often weak spots
Commitment and long-term persistence are a real test for you
results.relationship: In relationships you're witty and charming, forever bringing fresh energy and intellectual sparks. But you need a partner who can keep up with your pace and won't be rattled by your 'debate mode.' The keys to a lasting relationship are learning to holster your sharp wit when your partner needs comfort rather than analysis, and treating commitment as something worth sticking with.
results.career: You suit fields that call for creativity, variety, and persuasion: entrepreneurship, marketing, product development, consulting, law, media, and any work built on brainstorming. You come alive in free, ever-changing environments full of new things—and you despise unchanging routine and rigid bureaucracy.
results.growth: You've got ideas overflowing, but the world only remembers the few you actually 'finish.' Pick one or two you genuinely care about and practice seeing them all the way through—the ability to close the loop doubles your talent. Also, not every conversation needs to be won—sometimes the other person just wants to feel validated.

results.title: INFJ · Advocate
results.tagline: A quiet, resolute idealist who wants to leave the world a little better than they found it.
results.rarity: About 1% of the population—the rarest of all 16 types.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Introverted Intuition (Ni), sees the deeper meaning and direction behind people and events; your auxiliary function, Extraverted Feeling (Fe), perceives and responds to others' emotions with great sensitivity. Your tertiary Ti and inferior Se make you prize meaning over the sensory reality of the here and now.
results.description: You're a rare idealist—easygoing on the surface, but carrying a set of uncompromising values within. What you long for isn't just success, but meaning. You keenly read the emotions others leave unspoken, and often form an accurate gut read on someone the moment you meet. You're quiet, yet astonishingly steadfast—once you've settled on a conviction worth devoting yourself to, you'll spend a lifetime protecting it.
strengths: Profound insight—you see through to people's hearts and the essence of things
Powerful empathy and listening that leaves people feeling understood
Steadfast in your ideals and convictions, with the power to inspire and uplift others
Creative and altruistic in equal measure—valuing depth over surface
challenges: Prone to giving until you burn out, neglecting your own needs
Bottling up emotions until they hit a breaking point and explode (the threshold effect)
Chasing perfect ideals, easily disillusioned by reality
Overly sensitive—others' negative emotions often drain you
results.relationship: In relationships you seek soul-deep connection and can't stand shallowness or pretense. You give your whole heart to those you love, but you're easily let down when they fall short of the ideal in your mind. An essential lesson for sustaining your relationships is learning to voice your real needs and set healthy boundaries—rather than silently enduring and getting hurt alone.
results.career: You suit fields where you can live out your values and help others: counseling, education, writing, social work, nonprofits, human development, and creative work. You're most driven in meaningful, humane, autonomous environments—and can't tolerate purely mercenary, cold, or conscience-violating work.
results.growth: You're always catching others—but who catches you? Put taking care of yourself at the top of your list of ideals. Learn to set boundaries before you burn out and to speak your needs early, instead of silently shouldering it all until you break. Your gentleness is precious—don't save it only for the world and forget to save some for yourself.

results.title: INFP · Mediator
results.tagline: A gentle guardian of ideals with an entire universe inside—true to themselves and longing for authenticity.
results.rarity: About 4% of the population—overrepresented among artists and creators.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Introverted Feeling (Fi), anchors an extraordinarily deep and genuine set of personal values; your auxiliary function, Extraverted Intuition (Ne), sees the beautiful possibilities and potential in people and things. Your tertiary Si and inferior Te make real-world execution and objective decisiveness a struggle.
results.description: You feel things deeply and imagine vividly, with an entire universe living inside you. You detest pretense and conflict, longing for genuine connection and a meaning of your own. You seem easygoing, but touch your core values and you'll show astonishing steadfastness. You always find poetry in ordinary things, and you're often moved by other people's stories. For you, staying true to your inner reality matters more than living up to the world's expectations.
strengths: Sincere and kind, with deep empathy and acceptance
Rich imagination and a distinctive gift for creativity and aesthetics
Loyal to your personal values—not easily swept along with the crowd
Able to see others' potential and goodness, and skilled at lifting people up
challenges: When the gap between ideal and reality grows too wide, you slip into self-doubt and low spirits
You avoid conflict and everyday practicalities—follow-through tends to be weak
Overly hard on yourself, prone to overthinking and inner turmoil
You internalize emotions easily and aren't great at objective, decisive choices
results.relationship: In relationships you're romantic, devoted, and all in, seeking a soulmate you truly connect with. You'll accept everything about your partner, but you're easily hurt when the ideal shatters. Learning to tell 'the partner you imagined' from 'the partner who's actually there,' and to bravely voice your needs instead of quietly bearing it all, makes your love more grounded and lasting.
results.career: You suit fields where you can express yourself and connect with people's hearts: writing, art and design, counseling, education, social good, and any work that conveys meaning. You do your best in free, meaningful, respectful environments—and struggle most in fiercely competitive, cold, mercenary workplaces that force you to betray your true self.
results.growth: Your sensitivity isn't fragility—it's a gift for perceiving the subtle beauty of the world. But don't let 'the perfect version in your imagination' block 'the now you could begin'—break one small ideal into a tiny step you can take today. And be gentle with yourself; you judge yourself by a standard far harsher than the one you hold others to.

results.title: ENFJ · Protagonist
results.tagline: A charismatic guide who always makes the people around them feel seen and inspired.
results.rarity: About 2% of the population—common among teachers, leaders, and public figures.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Extraverted Feeling (Fe), precisely reads a group's emotions and needs and cultivates harmony; your auxiliary function, Introverted Intuition (Ni), sees each person's potential and possible future. Your tertiary Se and inferior Ti mean you occasionally struggle with purely objective, logic-based choices.
results.description: You're a born guide—charismatic, generous, and deeply infectious. You precisely sense a group's emotions and lead everyone to grow together, warmly yet firmly. Your sense of fulfillment comes from 'helping others become better versions of themselves.' You excel at rallying people and defusing conflict—the kind who lights up a room the moment you walk in and makes everyone feel valued. You believe in people's goodness and potential, and you'll go all out for it.
strengths: Powerful ability to inspire and rally—you motivate and unite people
Nuanced empathy and insight—skilled at reading and responding to others' needs
Generous and altruistic, glad to nurture others and elevate the team
Excellent communication and coordination—a natural bridge between people
challenges: You take on everyone's problems as your own, neglecting your own needs
Overly concerned with others' opinions, finding it hard to bear being disliked
You suppress your true opinions and emotions to keep the peace
You idealize others, then feel let down after all you've given
results.relationship: In relationships you're warm, considerate, and all in, always keeping your partner's needs top of mind. But you also tend to over-give and treat their happiness as your responsibility. Learning to accept that 'not everyone can be rescued by you,' and allowing yourself moments of vulnerability and being cared for too, keeps your relationships balanced instead of draining you dry.
results.career: You suit fields built on connecting with people and wielding influence: education, training, human resources, PR, leadership and management, counseling, and social good. You're most passionate in environments that inspire others and value collaboration and human care—and struggle most in cold, cutthroat, or isolating work climates.
results.growth: You give your light to everyone—remember to keep a lamp for yourself. Your worth isn't built on 'being liked by everyone'—learn to accept that some people just won't get you, and allow yourself a day of 'not wanting to take care of anyone.' Fill your own cup first, and you'll have an endless supply to pour for others.

results.title: ENFP · Campaigner
results.tagline: A walking generator of enthusiasm who can make an ordinary day feel like an adventure.
results.rarity: About 7% of the population—a fairly common and high-energy type.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Extraverted Intuition (Ne), is full of curiosity and possibility about everything in the world; your auxiliary function, Introverted Feeling (Fi), infuses everything with genuine meaning and value. Your tertiary Te and inferior Si make routine discipline and long-term attention to detail less of a strength.
results.description: You're free-spirited, passionate, and infectious—you can spot someone's spark in an instant and turn an ordinary day into an adventure. You crave deep connection and fresh experiences, and your worst fear is being trapped in an unchanging routine. Your curiosity is like a spring that never runs dry—there's always a new interest, a new plan, a new person to meet. You live life at full boil, and you generously infect everyone around you with that vitality.
strengths: Passionate and optimistic, with a powerful spark that ignites others
Boundless curiosity and creativity—you always see new possibilities
Sincere and empathetic, quick to build deep connections with people
Highly adaptable and bold to experiment—you turn life into an adventure
challenges: Prone to fleeting enthusiasm—chased around by novelty and struggling to focus
You hate routine and detail—execution and discipline are often lacking
Big emotional swings—easily drained by other people and your surroundings
Hard to say no and eager to please everyone, leaving you stretched too thin
results.relationship: In relationships you're romantic, passionate, and full of surprises, forever injecting fresh energy into the bond. But you also need to learn the patience to nurture the everyday once the honeymoon rush fades. The keys to your happiness are finding a partner who admires your freedom yet gives you a sense of stability, and practicing loving one person with focus.
results.career: You suit creative, ever-changing, people-facing fields: marketing and PR, creative planning, media, education, event planning, entrepreneurship, and any work that runs on passion and inspiration. You come alive in free, flexible, interaction-rich environments—and can't tolerate monotonous, isolating, rule-bound work.
results.growth: Your enthusiasm can light up the whole world—but don't forget to save some fuel for yourself. Practice the 'boring moments' of sticking with something after the excitement fades—that's the key to turning talent into achievement. And remember: you don't have to be good to everyone—save your most precious energy for the people and things that truly matter.

results.title: ISTJ · Logistician
results.tagline: The reliable backbone who does what they say—hand them the task and you can rest easy.
results.rarity: About 12% of the population—one of the most common types.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Introverted Sensing (Si), firmly internalizes past experience and facts into trustworthy standards; your auxiliary function, Extraverted Thinking (Te), plans and executes methodically. Your tertiary Fi and inferior Ne make you favor the familiar and the certain, and resist the unknown and sudden change.
results.description: You're a dependable backbone—practical, responsible, and true to your word, doing exactly what you say without any fuzziness. You believe in order, tradition, and honest effort; you're the person others can hand a task to and rest completely easy. You don't chase the spotlight—you just care about doing your part flawlessly. Your reliability isn't a gift but the sum of day-after-day self-discipline: while others run on inspiration, you run on discipline and experience.
strengths: A powerful sense of duty and follow-through—what you're assigned gets done, guaranteed
Meticulous and thorough—skilled at handling detail and safeguarding quality
Honest and reliable—you keep your word and steady the whole team
Practical and steady—you stay highly consistent over long-haul tasks
challenges: Cling to established methods, resisting change and new possibilities
Feel uneasy with abstract concepts and unexpected situations
Value rules so heavily you can seem rigid and inflexible
Not great at expressing emotion, which can leave those close to you feeling distant
results.relationship: In relationships you're loyal, stable, and deeply responsible, expressing love through concrete acts and dependable commitments. You're no good at romantic words, but you'll take care of your partner's life down to the last detail. Learning to occasionally break routine and surprise them, and to speak your feelings out loud, adds a layer of warmth on top of your rock-solid dependability.
results.career: You suit fields that demand precision, responsibility, and stability: accounting, finance, administration, legal affairs, engineering, quality control, data management, and any work that values process and detail. You do your best where the systems are clear, the goals are defined, and expertise and reputation are prized—and adapt worst to chaotic, ever-shifting organizations that change the rules on a whim.
results.growth: Your steadiness is the team's anchor, but the world moves faster than the rules. Try treating 'change' as a skill to practice, and occasionally allow yourself one attempt that doesn't follow the SOP. And don't let 'I'll just do it myself' bury your feelings—the people you care about really do want to hear you say a little more.

results.title: ISFJ · Defender
results.tagline: The warm guardian who holds everything up from behind the scenes—and rarely takes the credit.
results.rarity: About 13% of the population—the most common type.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Introverted Sensing (Si), remembers the little details of everyone's likes and needs; your auxiliary function, Extraverted Feeling (Fe), turns care into meticulous, concrete action. Your tertiary Ti and inferior Ne make you value stability and tradition, and shy away from risk.
results.description: You're a warm guardian—considerate, dutiful, and humble—always putting others' needs before your own and expressing love through actions rather than words. You value harmony and security, the kind who props everything up from behind the scenes and rarely takes credit. You quietly remember everyone's birthday, preferences, and struggles, and slip in to cover for them when they need it. Your giving is so natural that the people around you often only realize how important you are once you're gone.
strengths: Considerate and perceptive—you sense and tend to others' needs
A high sense of duty and great patience—grounded and dependable in your work
Loyal and warm—a trustworthy long-term partner
Attentive to detail and tradition—skilled at maintaining relationships and order
challenges: You over-sacrifice, can't say no, and swallow your grievances
Fearful of conflict and change, you easily miss out on what you truly want
Overly concerned with others' opinions, prone to anxiety and self-doubt
Your emotional needs often get suppressed—you're not good at speaking up for yourself
results.relationship: In relationships you're gentle, loyal, and selflessly devoted—the kind who can make a home and a bond incredibly warm. But you also tend to give without limit and neglect your own needs, even silently enduring unfairness. Learning to voice your feelings and needs, and to believe you deserve to be cared for too, is a crucial step toward healthy relationships.
results.career: You suit fields that care for others and attend to detail: nursing, education, administration, HR, customer service, social work, and any supportive work that calls for patience and empathy. You feel most at home in stable, harmonious environments where you're needed and appreciated—and struggle most in cold, high-conflict, or relentlessly competitive workplaces.
results.growth: You keep everyone in your heart—yet you're the one you most often leave out. Practice saying 'no'—turning people down won't make you a bad person, but it will let you live more like yourself. Your giving deserves to be seen and returned; don't be afraid to voice your needs—the people who truly love you will be glad to catch you.

results.title: ESTJ · Executive
results.tagline: A born organizer who swiftly untangles chaos and drives things to completion.
results.rarity: About 9% of the population—common in management and administrative roles.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Extraverted Thinking (Te), arranges people and resources in perfect order and chases results; your auxiliary function, Introverted Sensing (Si), leans on practical experience and established rules for steady judgment. Your tertiary Ne and inferior Fi make you less attuned to subtle emotions.
results.description: You're a born organizer—practical, decisive, and devoted to efficiency and order, able to swiftly untangle chaos and drive things to completion. You respect rules and commitments, mean what you say, and reward and discipline fairly, with a natural leadership instinct for getting a team running. You believe hard work and discipline eventually pay off, and you're willing to lead by example. In your eyes, the world should have clear rules and clear responsibilities—and you're the one who makes sure everything keeps running as it should.
strengths: Powerful organization and execution—you keep things running efficiently
Decisive and dependable—you dare to take responsibility and make the call when it counts
Value commitments and rules—consistent in word and deed, fair and clear
Practical and steady—skilled at building systems and maintaining order
challenges: Stubborn in your views, with little patience for different approaches
Overemphasize rules and efficiency, overlooking others' emotions and flexibility
Slow to accept criticism and change, coming across as domineering and dictatorial
Used to leading and controlling, sometimes drowning out the team's voice
results.relationship: In relationships you're reliable, responsible, and true to your word, giving your all to your duties toward family. But you also tend to treat love with a 'management' approach, easily overlooking your partner's emotional needs. Learning to listen before you judge, and to swap 'I think you should' for 'what would you like to do,' lets your care actually be felt.
results.career: You suit fields that call for organization, management, and decisiveness: business management, project leadership, administration, finance, military and police, law, and any work that values systems and efficiency. You do your best in goal-clear, reward-and-consequence-clear, results-driven environments—and can't tolerate lax, ambiguous, irresponsible organizations.
results.growth: Your ability to get things 'moving' is unmatched, but people aren't machines, and efficiency isn't the only answer. Try adding one more question—'what do you think?'—before you give the order, and leave room for others. Remember: the best leadership doesn't just get things done—it makes people want to follow.

results.title: ESFJ · Consul
results.tagline: The soul of the gathering and the caretaker of the friend group, making everyone feel welcome.
results.rarity: About 12% of the population—one of the most common types.
results.cognition: Your dominant function, Extraverted Feeling (Fe), keenly senses and responds to the emotions and needs of everyone around you and keeps the peace; your auxiliary function, Introverted Sensing (Si), turns care into one thoughtful, concrete act after another. Your tertiary Ne and inferior Ti make you lean on established values and others' approval.
results.description: You're the glue of the community—warm, responsible, and focused on relationships and belonging—the soul of the gathering and the caretaker of the friend group. You keenly sense everyone's emotions and always make people feel welcome and cared for. You find your worth in giving and being needed. You remember everyone's preferences, thoughtfully arrange every occasion, and tie people together tightly through concrete acts of care.
strengths: Warmhearted and considerate—skilled at caring for and rallying the people around you
Powerful organization and drive—you keep relationships and occasions running smoothly
Dutiful and reliable—you value commitments and tradition
Highly approachable—the group's social lubricant and mood-maker
challenges: Overly concerned with others' opinions, craving affirmation and approval
Fearful of conflict and rejection, easily wronging yourself to please others
Hard to take criticism—you magnify disapproval into self-doubt
Used to centering others, you overlook your own true needs
results.relationship: In relationships you're warm, loyal, and attentive to every detail, caring for your partner and family flawlessly. But you also tend to fret and over-give because you care so much what your partner thinks. Learning to tell 'I genuinely want to do this' from 'I'm doing this out of fear of being disliked,' and to believe you deserve love without earning it through people-pleasing, makes your relationships lighter and more at ease.
results.career: You suit fields built on serving others and cultivating relationships: education, nursing, customer service, HR, events and administration, PR, and any work that calls for warmth and coordination. You're most driven in harmonious environments where you're needed and get clear interpersonal feedback—and struggle most in cold, isolating, or highly adversarial workplaces.
results.growth: You make every relationship warm and moving, but your worth doesn't need others' approval to prove it. Practice telling 'I genuinely want to do this' from 'I'm doing this out of fear of being disliked'—the former makes you shine, the latter slowly hollows you out. The love you deserve doesn't have to be bought with people-pleasing.

results.title: ISTP · Virtuoso
